jobs.title: Integrated Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
At Pursuit Spirits, we’re redefining the whiskey experience. Born from the creators of the award\-winning Bourbon Pursuit podcast, we blend our deep industry knowledge with bold creativity to craft unique and high\-quality American whiskeys that have captured the hearts of enthusiasts. We make whiskey for those who know good whiskey.

As a small dynamic team, we’re creating a movement. With our new visitor experience on Louisville’s historic Whiskey Row, we offer immersive one\-of\-a\-kind experiences, hands\-on educational sessions, a retail shop and a transformative cocktail and tasting bar for more people to join the pursuit.

**Our Core Values**

* **We are obsessed with customer success.** Our growth is anchored by every distributor, supplier and retail customer that believes in our pursuit.
* **We don't take ourselves too seriously but we do seriously good work.** Our passion delivers excellence by n improving ourselves professionally and personally and we have fun in the pursuit of making moments for others.
* **We defy convention and are intentional in action.** We continually reimagine ourselves in pursuit of what is next.
* **We don't see problems, only solutions.** We rely on each other as a team and are quick to resolve issues, while being empowered to make the best decisions with good intentions to further our pursuit.
* **We may not agree but we're honest and build trust through accountability.** We value diverse perspectives and take collective responsibility, while holding ourselves and each other accountable, in the lol of our shared goals.

**Why Should You Come Work With Us?**

* **Be a Trailblazer:** We aren’t some stodgy corporation. Work alongside a very small team of passionate individuals who want to shape the future of the whiskey experience in a fast\-paced startup.
* **Growth and Creativity:** You won’t be stuck in a box. Collaborate in a fast\-growing, dynamic company that encourages new ideas and creative freedom to discover new solutions.
* **Best In Class:** We don’t mess with mediocre. Play an integral part in delivering unforgettable moments for whiskey lovers through our products, storytelling, and visitor experience.
* **Enjoy the Perks:** Join a team that values work\-life balance with opportunities for professional growth.

**The Role**

Pursuit Spirits is seeking an IntegratedMarketing Manager to lead the strategic and creative growth of our brand portfolio while curating captivating activations. This individual will act as the bridge between our production team, our visitor experience at Whiskey Row, and our growing national audience. You will be responsible for enforcing the brand’s voice, managing our public presence, and creating high\-impact experiences that drive both foot traffic and sales.

**Brand Leadership**

* Maintain and enforce brand standards and positioning for Pursuit Spirits, Trial \+ Error, and the Whiskey Row Visitor Experience to ensure 360\-degree consistency of our identity.
* Guide the visual and narrative direction across digital, print, merchandise, and on\-site distillery experiences.
* Partner with sales, production, and executive leadership to ensure all marketing efforts reflect company\-wide priorities and inventory goals.
* Research and lead the submission process for spirit and traveler awards to amplify marketing and PR efforts.

**Event Programming**

* Design and execute a year\-round activation calendar for Trial \+ Error, including pairings, holiday events, and launch parties to drive consistent traffic.
* Cultivate high\-value relationships with local hotels, tourism boards, convention bureaus, influencers, and businesses to keep Pursuit at the top of the Louisville "must\-visit" list.
* Assist in the conceptualization and design coordination of merchandise for the Whiskey Row experience to enhance the guest "take\-home" journey.

**Growth \& Digital Marketing**

* Proactively identify new market opportunities across local, destination, and distributed regions.
* Manage the Mailchimp lifecycle, including newsletter creation and automated communications to our database of subscribers.
* Manage the Trial \+ Error social media ecosystem, including content strategy, posting schedules, and active community engagement.

**Creative Production \& PR**

* Independently produce polished, on\-brand marketing assets using Adobe Creative Suite or Canva when speed or budget requires "hands\-on" work.
* Maintain a library of templates and act as the primary point of contact for external agencies or designers on large\-scale creative projects.
* Draft and distribute press releases to media databases and industry contacts to maintain a consistent news cycle.

**Qualifications**

**Experience**:

* 4–7 years in brand management, marketing, or creative direction, ideally within the spirits (distilleries), wine, or hospitality industries.
* A background working in a creative agency or as an in\-house "solo" marketer where they were responsible for high\-speed content production.

**Skills**:

* Exceptional written and verbal communication abilities.
* Familiarity in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) or Canva for quick projects.
* Experience managing Meta Business Suite (Instagram/Facebook ads) and a working knowledge of Google Ads, Yelp, and TripAdvisor for local tourism traffic.
* Expertise in Mailchimp or similar CRM tools, including list segmentation and designing high\-performing newsletters.
* The ability to switch between "distillery brand voice" for press releases and "casual enthusiast voice" for social media.
* Proven ability to manage a year\-round calendar of overlapping events, award deadlines, and product launches without losing track of details.
* A strategic thinker that can take top level objectives and turn them into actionable plans.

**Other Requirements**:

* Flexibility to work nights and weekends based on events and other needs.
* Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Graphic Design, Business, or Communications.

**Benefits**

* Competitive base salary with a quarterly bonus based on achieving goals.
* A hybrid role where presence at Pursuit Spirits locations is required two to three days per week.
* Paid time off
* Opportunity to lead and grow the marketing presence for Pursuit Spirits.
* Join a dynamic and passionate team in a rapidly growing company.

If you are a visionary storyteller, a versatile creator with a passion for building premium brands, and a savant at throwing great events, we want to hear from you. We are looking for a strategic thinker who isn’t afraid to get their hands dirty and who can push our creative direction one hour, design a high\-impact campaign the next, and can put together activations with spunk. Apply today to join the Pursuit Spirits team and help us define the next chapter of Pursuit!

Pursuit Spirits is an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive work environment.

Pay: $70,000\.00 \- $85,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* Employee discount
* Flexible schedule
* Happy hour
* Paid time off

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Louisville, KY 40206

jobs.title: Senior Campaign Manager
jobs.description:
Are you passionate about building marketing campaigns that truly make an impact? We’re looking for an experienced **Senior Campaign Manager** to join our growing team in **Cary, NC**, where you’ll take the lead in developing high\-performing marketing initiatives that drive brand awareness, customer engagement, and measurable business growth.

This position is ideal for a strategic marketer who enjoys owning campaigns from concept to execution. As a Senior Campaign Manager, you will oversee the planning, delivery, and optimization of integrated marketing campaigns across multiple channels while collaborating with internal teams and external partners. Your work will directly influence how our brand connects with audiences and delivers results in a competitive marketplace.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

In this role, you will take ownership of a broad range of marketing initiatives, ensuring campaigns are effectively designed, delivered, and optimized for performance. Key responsibilities include:

* Leading the end\-to\-end management of marketing campaigns, from planning and concept development to launch and performance analysis
* Designing integrated campaign strategies across digital, social, email, and content marketing channels
* Collaborating closely with internal teams including marketing, sales, creative, and analytics to ensure campaigns align with broader business goals
* Monitoring campaign performance using analytics tools and marketing metrics, identifying opportunities for improvement and optimization
* Managing campaign timelines, deliverables, and budgets to ensure projects remain on track and deliver value
* Developing targeted messaging and campaign frameworks that resonate with key customer audiences

The Experience We’re Looking For

We’re seeking a marketing professional who combines strategic thinking with strong project leadership skills. The ideal candidate will bring:

* Several years of experience managing marketing campaigns, integrated marketing programs, or brand initiatives
* A strong understanding of digital marketing channels, campaign planning, and performance tracking
* Proven ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work across teams and departments
* Experience interpreting data and analytics to guide campaign decisions
* A proactive approach to problem solving and continuous campaign improvement

Candidates with experience in marketing automation platforms, CRM systems, and campaign analytics tools will be particularly well suited to this role.

Why Join Our Team

We believe successful campaigns start with great people. By joining our team in Cary, you’ll benefit from:

* The opportunity to lead high\-impact marketing campaigns that influence business growth
* A collaborative environment where new ideas and strategic thinking are encouraged
* Access to resources and support that allow you to expand your marketing leadership and campaign management skills
* A workplace culture that values creativity, innovation, and professional development
* Long\-term career opportunities within a growing and ambitious organization

Interested in Applying?

If you’re an experienced campaign professional who enjoys developing strategy, managing complex marketing initiatives, and delivering measurable results, we’d be excited to hear from you.

Submit your application to be considered for this opportunity. Our recruitment team will review applications and contact shortlisted candidates to discuss the next stage of the hiring process.

Pay: $65,693\.85 \- $71,093\.67 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Dental insurance
* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Congressional Campaign Manager
jobs.description:
**Job Overview**  
**Tim Arnous for Congress** is a grassroots campaign in California’s 50th District seeking an experienced Campaign Manager to join our disciplined, mission\-driven team for the 2026 election.

This role requires sound political judgment, operational discipline, and the ability to translate strategy into execution in a fast\-paced campaign environment. The Campaign Manager will oversee daily operations, coordinate staff and volunteers, and ensure the campaign remains organized, strategic, and results\-focused.

Learn more: **timforsandiego.com**

**Application Requirement**

A cover letter and 3 references are required.

Use of AI to write a cover letter will result in application denial.

**Ideal Candidate**

* Exceptional attention to detail
* Excellent communication skills
* Attentive and thoughtful listener
* Experience managing campaigns, teams, and volunteers
* Highly organized with strong operational discipline
* Results\-driven with the ability to adjust strategy when needed
* Strategic thinker with sound political judgment
* Knowledge of FEC compliance and campaign reporting requirements
* Familiarity with contracts and campaign operations

This position requires professionalism, accountability, and the ability to lead with clarity and focus in a demanding campaign environment.

Pay: $51,036\.82 \- $61,463\.70 per year

Experience:

* political committee : 2 years (Required)

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Field Marketing Representative
jobs.description:
**Core Responsibilities:**

* **Business\-to\-Business (B2B) Interactions:** The role focuses on engaging with other businesses in the healthcare sector. This involves contacting potential clients, such as hospitals, clinics, or medical practices, to introduce them to us.
* **Referrals:** A key component of the job is to generate referrals. This means that you’ll need to build relationships with current providers and leverage these connections to obtain new client referrals.

**Strong Communication Skills:**

You should be able to articulate our services in a clear and persuasive manner. This involves both verbal and written communication skills.

**Experience in Medical Sales or the Healthcare Industry:**

Familiarity with sales processes, client management, industry\-specific strategies, and knowledge of the healthcare environment helps in understanding each client's needs and navigating industry\-specific challenges.

**Knowledge of ABA Services:**

Knowing the services offered, how they are delivered, and their impact on healthcare delivery is important.

**Ability to Build and Maintain Client Relationships:**

* **Relationship Building:** Developing strong, trust\-based relationships with clients is essential. This involves regular communication, follow\-ups, and providing excellent customer service.
* **Client Retention:** Maintaining these relationships over time to ensure repeat business and referrals is a key aspect of the role.

**Additional Info:**

* **Remote Work:** As a remote role, you'll need to be comfortable working independently and managing your time effectively without direct supervision.
* **Self\-Motivation:** Success in this role will largely depend on your ability to stay motivated and driven, especially since you’ll be working remotely.
* **Technological Proficiency:** Familiarity with virtual communication tools, CRM software, and other remote work technologies will be important for day\-to\-day operations.

In summary, this role requires a blend of sales expertise, industry knowledge, and strong interpersonal skills to effectively engage with healthcare businesses and generate valuable referrals.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $25\.00 \- $35\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule
* Health insurance
* Work from home

Work Location: On the road

jobs.title: Field Marketing Coordinator
jobs.description:
LGI Homes is seeking a Field Marketing Coordinator in our Las Vegas, Nevada market.

  

As one of the World’s Most Trustworthy Companies and a Top Workplace in the USA, LGI Homes has a notable legacy of homebuilding excellence. We are seeking marketing professionals who are self\-motivated and eager to dive into the LGI Homes system and culture.

  

The Field Marketing Coordinator brings LGI Homes marketing to life across multiple communities by planning and executing community events, showcases, grand openings, and Realtor engagements that drive traffic and enhance the customer experience. This field\-based role activates national campaigns locally, improves community presentation, supports content creation, and provides insights that help drive sales performance.

  

As the on\-the\-ground extension of the marketing team, this role ensures signage, collateral, digital listings, and customer touchpoints are polished, consistent, and aligned with brand standards. A major part of the role includes managing the full event process—planning, coordination, onsite execution, and post\-event follow\-up—to maintain strong community visibility and engagement.

  

The coordinator also handles data entry and reporting, tracking traffic trends, reviewing online listings, evaluating sign routes, and sharing weekly insights with leadership. Additional responsibilities include managing marketing inventory and vendors, supporting photography and video needs, assisting with community launches and closeouts, and ensuring information centers are always visitor\-ready.

 **Requirements**  

* Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field, with 2\+ years of experience in marketing, events, or community engagement
* Strong event planning, project management, and communication skills
* Creativity and an eye for presentation, with the ability to manage multiple priorities
* Strong social media and content creation skills
* Digital proficiency with social media management and content editing tools
* Comfortable traveling between communities and working evenings and weekends for events
* Skilled at building strong relationships with teams, partners, and the community

  

**Benefits**  

In addition to a competitive compensation package, this position offers comprehensive training and exceptional benefits which include: medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with 4% match, a paid car and phone allowance, an employee stock purchase plan and a new home discount. We also boast a rich company culture focused on training, goals, and recognition.

 **Compensation**: $55,000 \- $80,000, Annual Bonus Opportunities up to 10%

jobs.description:
Overview: The Creative Execution Campaign manager is responsible for ensuring the highest standards of Multicultural brand advertising and creative content across a variety of campaigns, producing compelling omnichannel content, and managing the end\-to\-end creative process. This senior individual contributor will oversee Spanish\-language creative campaign elements, route content for key stakeholder and legal reviews, collaborate with internal and external partners and/or agencies, and lead day\-to\-day activity tied to creative agency projects to deliver impactful marketing initiatives.**Job Summary:**
In addition to the responsibilities listed below, this position is responsible for developing the development and implementation of complex tools, assets, insights and guidance necessary to deploy consistent, effective and differentiated brand messaging across all communication touch points and experiences, aimed at increasing brand equity, awareness and preference.

 **Essential Responsibilities:*** Practices self\-leadership and promotes learning in others by building relationships with cross\-functional stakeholders; communicating information and providing advice to drive projects forward; influencing team members within assigned unit; listening and responding to, seeking, and addressing performance feedback; adapting to competing demands and new responsibilities; providing feedback to others, including upward feedback to leadership and mentoring junior team members; creating and executing plans to capitalize on strengths and develop weaknesses; and adapting to and learning from change, difficulties, and feedback.
* Conducts or oversees business\-specific projects by applying deep expertise in subject area; promoting adherence to all procedures and policies; developing work plans to meet business priorities and deadlines; determining and carrying out processes and methodologies; coordinating and delegating resources to accomplish organizational goals; partnering internally and externally to make effective business decisions; solving complex problems; escalating issues or risks as appropriate; monitoring progress and results; recognizing and capitalizing on improvement opportunities; and evaluating recommendations made by others.
* Leads the development and implementation of strategic go\-to\-market plans by managing relationships with channel partners; assessing and validating channel partner needs and performance on an ongoing basis; investigating audience needs using data\-driven learnings to evaluate and identify new multi\-channel opportunities; and leading channel marketing programs to drive go\-to\-market strategies.
* Leads the design and implementation of complex marketing strategies by ensuring strategies support membership growth, retention, and product/service promotion initiatives to meet business goals; developing strategic plan deliverables; verifying brand consistency and brand standard compliance through all marketing channels; conducting and leveraging market research to identify new opportunities and/or validate assumptions; analyzing sales and marketing metrics; creating reports and presenting results; tracking progress to overall enterprise objectives; and driving improvement and optimization efforts toward established goals.
* Manages and drives complex marketing projects by developing and updating project plans; identifying and managing cross\-functional stakeholder contacts; assembling teams based on project needs and team member strengths; forecasting and adhering to project budget; evaluating and selecting vendors; defining scope of vendor contracts; managing vendor relationships; and presenting project updates.
* Provides direction to creative team by gathering data, research, and customer input and sign\-off to develop an understanding of the work; collaborating with communication and marketing teams; developing and writing complete creative briefs that inform and inspire writers, designers, and other creative professionals; reviewing the completed brief with the customer and relevant stakeholders; and presenting the brief.
* Leads and drives the development and execution of integrated marketing communications programs and strategies by integrating business\-to\-business, business\-to\-consumer, and line of business; analyzing information from market research teams to understand stakeholder attitudes and needs; translating insights into promotional strategies; working with enterprise teams to align activities; communicating consistent positioning and messages across all media; evaluating effectiveness of plans, programs, and campaigns; and making recommendations to optimize campaigns.
* Leads and conducts complex market data collection and synthesis by interpreting data and insights to turn them into actionable, evidence\-based marketing plans; partnering with research and strategy teams to gather marketplace and consumer dynamics to drive marketing strategies; defining market research needs; partnering with analytics teams to drive marketing strategies and track performance; and reporting and optimizing ROI on strategies and tactics.

 **Knowledge, Skills and Abilities: (Core)*** Ambiguity/Uncertainty Management
* Attention to Detail
* Business Knowledge
* Communication
* Critical Thinking
* Cross\-Group Collaboration
* Decision Making
* Dependability
* Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion Support
* Drives Results
* Facilitation Skills
* Health Care Industry
* Influencing Others
* Integrity
* Learning Agility
* Organizational Savvy
* Problem Solving
* Short\- and Long\-term Learning \& Recall
* Teamwork
* Topic\-Specific Communication

 **Knowledge, Skills and Abilities: (Functional)*** Brand Identity/Creative Design
* Brand Strategy
* Analytical Skills
* Business Planning
* Digital Marketing
* Emotional Intelligence
* Financial Acumen
* Getting Work Done Through Others
* Marketing
* Negotiation
* Project Management
* Social Media
* Strategic Alignment
* Strategic Program Management
* Vendor Management
* Written Communication

 **Minimum Qualifications:*** Minimum six (6\) years brand management experience, including at least Minimum two (2\) years brand strategy experience.
* Minimum three (3\) years experience in a leadership role with or without direct reports.
* Bachelors degree in Marketing, Integrated Marketing Communications, Journalism, Humanities, Business, Social Science, or related field and Minimum eight (8\) years experience in marketing, communications, or a directly related field. Additional equivalent work experience in a directly related field may be substituted for the degree requirement.

**Additional Requirements:**

* Bilingual applicant required.

**Preferred Qualifications:*** Three (3\) years experience in marketing analysis.
* Two (2\) years experience managing operational or project budgets.

jobs.title: Field Marketing Manager - New Home Construction
jobs.description:
**Location:** Arlington, VA (hybrid @ our HQs with heavy regional travel)

**Team:** Marketing

**Reports to:** VP of Marketing, Policy \& Regulatory

**Type:** Full\-time (exempt)

**Job details**
---------------


Dandelion Energy is seeking a driven, strategic **Field Marketer** to serve as our on\-the\-ground expert and brand ambassador in on the East Coast, focused on supporting our largest homebuilder partners.


This mid\-level role blends **relationship building, sales enablement, localized marketing strategy, and hands\-on event execution**. You will work directly with builder sales teams to ensure homes equipped with Dandelion geothermal systems are successfully marketed and sold to homebuyers.


You’ll also play a key role in **educating and exciting the broader ecosystem**—including homebuyers, realtors, and local partners—through events, trainings, and community engagement that help make geothermal the industry standard for new home construction.


This role is **full\-time with extensive travel (50%\+) across the Eastern U.S.** While the position offers flexibility, it is not a traditional 9–5 role and will include occasional evenings and weekends to support events and builder activities.

**Responsibilities**
--------------------

**Builder Relationship Management \& Sales Enablement**

* Build deep, trusted relationships with homebuilder sales teams.
* Ensure builder teams have the training, resources, and collateral needed to confidently educate homebuyers about geothermal systems.
* Develop localized strategies and tools to help builders successfully market geothermal homes.

**Field Marketing \& Event Activation**

* Represent Dandelion at builder events, realtor gatherings, and homebuyer education events.
* Plan and execute local activations that generate awareness, excitement, and demand for geothermal\-powered homes.
* Serve as a brand ambassador for Dandelion within builder communities and local housing ecosystems.

**Strategy \& Collateral Development**

* Support field marketing strategy for the new\-construction vertical across the Eastern U.S.
* Create, localize, and manage execution of brochures, point\-of\-sale materials, signage, digital assets, and presentations tailored to builders and homebuyers.

**Market Feedback \& Intelligence**

* Serve as the “voice of the homebuyer” and builder partner, gathering real\-world insights from the field.
* Capture feedback from builder teams, homebuyers, and partners to inform marketing strategy, messaging, and product development.
* Provide actionable insights to Dandelion’s Marketing, Sales, and Product teams to improve go\-to\-market effectiveness.

**Travel** up to 50%\+ across the Mid\-Atlantic/Northeast to work with builder partners and support events.

**We’re looking for:**
----------------------

* Energized by being customer\-facing and building strong relationships.
* A confident presenter who can effectively engage builders, realtors, and homebuyers.
* Curious about the market and motivated to gather on\-the\-ground insights that shape strategy.
* Highly self\-directed, resourceful, and comfortable figuring things out as you go.
* Flexible and excited by a role that includes travel and occasional evening or weekend events.
* Passionate about clean energy, sustainability, or climate solutions (professional experience in these areas is a plus).

**Must****\-haves**
-------------------

* 3–5 years of experience in **field marketing, channel marketing, sales enablement, events marketing, or builder marketing**.
* 2\+ years experience building measurable marketing programs that drive awareness and sales outcomes.
* Exceptional presentation and public speaking skills.
* Proven relationship\-building ability with external partners (B2B2C a bonus).
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to travel extensively (50%\+).
* Bachelor’s degree

**Bonus points for:**
---------------------

* 2\+ years experience in home construction, real estate, working with homebuilders, and/or high\-value B2C tech.
* 1\+ years experience driving demand for a new or niche category.
* Field marketing in a builder or developer ecosystem.
* Experience in clean energy, sustainability and/or climate solutions.
* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Communications or similar discipline

**About Dandelion**
-------------------


Dandelion Energy is transforming how homes are heated and cooled—starting from (below) the ground up. As the nation’s leading residential geothermal company, we design, install, and maintain all\-in\-one heating and cooling systems that are cleaner, quieter, more affordable, and up to 4× more efficient than traditional HVAC. Our focus is on large, enterprise deployments with major homebuilders, helping bring geothermal to more homes, faster.


Backed by Google Ventures (GV) and other top tech investors, our end\-to\-end geothermal solution is challenging the status quo in residential heating and cooling. We’re scaling strategically and intentionally to meet growing demand. Our mission is simple and bold: to make geothermal the default choice for heating and cooling in the U.S.

**Why Dandelion**
-----------------


Dandelion Energy is at the forefront of the transition to clean, efficient home energy. Our team has delivered thousands of high\-quality geothermal installations across the country, helped elevate geothermal heat pumps nationally, and played a meaningful role in advancing electrification\-friendly policy at both the local and federal levels.


We’re a multidisciplinary, mission\-driven team spanning hardware engineering, HVAC, drilling, solar finance, behavioral economics, and startup operations. Together, we’re building something complex, impactful, and designed to scale … work that directly shapes how homes are powered for decades to come.


At Dandelion, you’ll find a collaborative, fast\-paced, and inclusive culture where curiosity, ownership, and cross\-functional teamwork are core to how we operate. Our nationwide team is united by a shared commitment to sustainability and a belief that clean, affordable heating and cooling should be the standard.


We’re driving a wholesale shift in how homes are heated and cooled. **Join us.**

**Benefits \& Perks**
---------------------

* Medical (including mental health), dental, vision, and pet insurance
* 401(k) plan
* Commuter benefits
* Generous paid sick leave, vacation, and parental leave
* Learning \& development support, including on\-the\-job training and virtual courses
* Pet insurance

jobs.title: Field Marketing Manager - New Home Construction
jobs.description:
**Location:** Denver, CO area (remote with heavy local/regional travel)

**Team:** Marketing

**Reports to:** VP of Marketing, Policy \& Regulatory

**Type:** Full\-time (exempt)

**Job details**
---------------


Dandelion Energy is seeking a driven, strategic **Field Marketer** to serve as our on\-the\-ground expert and brand ambassador in on the East Coast, focused on supporting our largest homebuilder partners.


This mid\-level role blends **relationship building, sales enablement, localized marketing strategy, and hands\-on event execution**. You will work directly with builder sales teams to ensure homes equipped with Dandelion geothermal systems are successfully marketed and sold to homebuyers.


You’ll also play a key role in **educating and exciting the broader ecosystem**—including homebuyers, realtors, and local partners—through events, trainings, and community engagement that help make geothermal the industry standard for new home construction.


This role is **full\-time with extensive travel (50%\+) across Colorado.** While the position offers flexibility, it is not a traditional 9–5 role and will include occasional evenings and weekends to support events and builder activities.

**Responsibilities**
--------------------

**Builder Relationship Management \& Sales Enablement**

* Build deep, trusted relationships with homebuilder sales teams.
* Ensure builder teams have the training, resources, and collateral needed to confidently educate homebuyers about geothermal systems.
* Develop localized strategies and tools to help builders successfully market geothermal homes.

**Field Marketing \& Event Activation**

* Represent Dandelion at builder events, realtor gatherings, and homebuyer education events.
* Plan and execute local activations that generate awareness, excitement, and demand for geothermal\-powered homes.
* Serve as a brand ambassador for Dandelion within builder communities and local housing ecosystems.

**Strategy \& Collateral Development**

* Support field marketing strategy for the new\-construction vertical across the Eastern U.S.
* Create, localize, and manage execution of brochures, point\-of\-sale materials, signage, digital assets, and presentations tailored to builders and homebuyers.

**Market Feedback \& Intelligence**

* Serve as the “voice of the homebuyer” and builder partner, gathering real\-world insights from the field.
* Capture feedback from builder teams, homebuyers, and partners to inform marketing strategy, messaging, and product development.
* Provide actionable insights to Dandelion’s Marketing, Sales, and Product teams to improve go\-to\-market effectiveness.

**Travel** up to 50%\+ across Colorado/midwest to work with builder partners and support events.

**We’re looking for:**
----------------------

* Energized by being customer\-facing and building strong relationships.
* A confident presenter who can effectively engage builders, realtors, and homebuyers.
* Curious about the market and motivated to gather on\-the\-ground insights that shape strategy.
* Highly self\-directed, resourceful, and comfortable figuring things out as you go.
* Flexible and excited by a role that includes travel and occasional evening or weekend events.
* Passionate about clean energy, sustainability, or climate solutions (professional experience in these areas is a plus).

**Must****\-haves**
-------------------

* 3–5 years of experience in **field marketing, channel marketing, sales enablement, events marketing, or builder marketing**.
* 2\+ years experience building measurable marketing programs that drive awareness and sales outcomes.
* Exceptional presentation and public speaking skills.
* Proven relationship\-building ability with external partners (B2B2C a bonus).
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to travel extensively (50%\+).
* Bachelor’s degree

**Bonus points for:**
---------------------

* 2\+ years experience in home construction, real estate, working with homebuilders, and/or high\-value B2C tech.
* 1\+ years experience driving demand for a new or niche category.
* Field marketing in a builder or developer ecosystem.
* Experience in clean energy, sustainability and/or climate solutions.
* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Communications or similar discipline

**About Dandelion**
-------------------


Dandelion Energy is transforming how homes are heated and cooled—starting from (below) the ground up. As the nation’s leading residential geothermal company, we design, install, and maintain all\-in\-one heating and cooling systems that are cleaner, quieter, more affordable, and up to 4× more efficient than traditional HVAC. Our focus is on large, enterprise deployments with major homebuilders, helping bring geothermal to more homes, faster.


Backed by Google Ventures (GV) and other top tech investors, our end\-to\-end geothermal solution is challenging the status quo in residential heating and cooling. We’re scaling strategically and intentionally to meet growing demand. Our mission is simple and bold: to make geothermal the default choice for heating and cooling in the U.S.

**Why Dandelion**
-----------------


Dandelion Energy is at the forefront of the transition to clean, efficient home energy. Our team has delivered thousands of high\-quality geothermal installations across the country, helped elevate geothermal heat pumps nationally, and played a meaningful role in advancing electrification\-friendly policy at both the local and federal levels.


We’re a multidisciplinary, mission\-driven team spanning hardware engineering, HVAC, drilling, solar finance, behavioral economics, and startup operations. Together, we’re building something complex, impactful, and designed to scale … work that directly shapes how homes are powered for decades to come.


At Dandelion, you’ll find a collaborative, fast\-paced, and inclusive culture where curiosity, ownership, and cross\-functional teamwork are core to how we operate. Our nationwide team is united by a shared commitment to sustainability and a belief that clean, affordable heating and cooling should be the standard.


We’re driving a wholesale shift in how homes are heated and cooled. **Join us.**

**Benefits \& Perks**
---------------------

* Medical (including mental health), dental, vision, and pet insurance
* 401(k) plan
* Commuter benefits
* Generous paid sick leave, vacation, and parental leave
* Learning \& development support, including on\-the\-job training and virtual courses
* Pet insurance

jobs.description:

Salary\-PG\-18


Weekly Wage access is an option for this position


**Job Summary:** Responsible for all activities related to conceptualizing, developing market goals, strategies and measurements to achieve marketing targets. This position will report to the Senior Marketing Director.


**Primary Tasks:**

* You will evaluate regional market and economic trends, and introduce appropriate strategic changes to exceed marketing expectations, maximize our reach to our core demographic and support our strategic goals.
* Plan regional advertising promotions through design and graphic arts to accomplish marketing policies and strategies of the organization, create increased revenue and ensure competitive position in the gaming market.
* You will build regional departmental projects from the conceptual phase to implementation and closeout following appropriate project management methodologies including organizational communication to other impacted areas.
* Develop the annual regional marketing plan and the measurable goals, strategies and resources necessary to achieve overall goals, including increasing brand awareness and market share, new customer acquisition and retention.
* You will develop the annual regional budget according to an approved annual marketing plan; oversee expenses to maintain acceptable margins; approve invoices for payment; review budget reports monthly.
* Communicate regional marketing plans across the organization to implement promotional events to enhance casino profitability.
* You will exercise knowledgeable leadership by example and through coaching the marketing team.
* Develop and implement regional procedures to eliminate problems, increase efficiency, maintain adequate internal controls and ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
* Resolve regional customer or associate disputes promptly that exceeds guest experience expectations.

Perform other duties as assigned.  

* 
**Requirements:**

Bachelor’s degree in marketing, Business Administration, or 4 years directly related experience.


General computer application proficiency (spreadsheets (can maintain complex spreadsheets),).


Advertising experience


Apply oral or written (legible) communication techniques.


Understand P\&L statement and how your actions impact the team.


Seven years of marketing management experience with direct responsibility for the marketing budget and all marketing operations.

jobs.title: ABM Operations & Execution Intern - Summer 2026
jobs.description:
Snowflake is about empowering enterprises to achieve their full potential — and people too. With a culture that’s all in on impact, innovation, and collaboration, Snowflake is the sweet spot for building big, moving fast, and taking technology — and careers — to the next level.


There is only one Data Cloud. Snowflake’s founders started from scratch and designed a data platform built for the cloud that is effective, affordable, and accessible to all data users. But it didn’t stop there. They engineered Snowflake to power the Data Cloud, where thousands of organizations unlock the value of their data with near\-unlimited scale, concurrency, and performance. This is our vision: a world with endless insights to tackle the challenges and opportunities of today and reveal the possibilities of tomorrow.


We’re looking for dedicated students who share our passion for ground\-breaking technology and want to create a lasting future for you and Snowflake.
  

  

Snowflake is looking for a high\-energy, technically\-minded ABM Operations \& Execution Intern to support our Account\-Based Marketing team. This role is unique, offering a dual focus on the creative execution of 1:1 and 1:few marketing campaigns and the technical infrastructure that powers them. You will help drive efficiency, scalability, and data\-driven insights across our most strategic enterprise accounts.

**WHAT WE OFFER :**

* Paid, full\-time internships in the heart of the software industry
* Post\-internship career opportunities (full\-time and/or additional internships)
* Exposure to a fast\-paced, fun and inclusive culture
* A chance to work with world\-class experts on challenging projects
* Opportunity to provide meaningful contributions to a real system used by customers
* High level of access to supervisors (manager and mentor), detailed direction without micromanagement, feedback throughout your internship, and a final evaluation

**WHAT WE EXPECT :**

* Must be actively enrolled in an accredited college/university program during the time of the internship
* Desired Class Level: Pursuing a Bachelors/Masters degree

**WHAT YOU WILL LEARN / GAIN :**

* Campaign Execution Support: Assist in the planning and execution of multi\-touch, multi\-channel ABM programs, including custom digital landing pages, targeted advertising, and account\-specific research.
* Automation \& Orchestration: Support the development and maintenance of operational workflows using tools like Workato to ensure seamless data flow between systems.
* Marketing Technology Management: Assist in managing and integrating ABM platforms with our marketing automation and CRM systems, primarily Marketo and Salesforce.
* Data Analysis: Help build and maintain dashboards to track campaign ROI and performance, providing recommendations for optimization.
* Process Improvement: Identify opportunities to enhance ABM program efficiency and help document new operational procedures.

**WHAT YOU SHOULD HAVE:**

* Technical Interest: A strong interest in or foundational knowledge of technical automation tools like Workato and marketing automation platforms like Marketo.
* Marketing Curiosity: A genuine interest in the "why" behind marketing strategies and how those workflows drive the customer journey and impact the bottom line.
* Analytical Mindset: Proficiency in or an eagerness to learn technical reporting and data visualization tools (e.g., Sigma, Snowflake, or Streamlit).
* Creative Problem Solving: The ability to think creatively about messaging and targeting for high\-value enterprise accounts.
* Attention to Detail: Maniacal attention to detail and strong project management skills to handle competing priorities in a fast\-paced environment.
* Communication: Strong interpersonal skills to facilitate collaboration between ABM team members and sales stakeholders.
* Emerging Tech Aptitude: An interest in the rapid adoption of AI in Marketing and a desire to learn how to implement AI/ML models within marketing workflows.

*Every Snowflake employee is expected to follow the company’s confidentiality and security standards for handling sensitive data. Snowflake employees must abide by the company’s data security plan as an essential part of their duties. It is every employee's duty to keep customer information secure and confidential.*


Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.


How do you want to make your impact?


For jobs located in the United States, please visit the job posting on the Snowflake Careers Site for salary and benefits information: careers.snowflake.com


The following represents the expected range of compensation for this role:

* The estimated pay range for this role is $26\.00 \- $30\.00 per hour.


The successful candidate’s starting hourly rate will be determined based on permissible, non\-discriminatory factors such as skills, experience, and geographic location. This role is also eligible for a competitive benefits package that includes: medical, dental, vision, life, and disability insurance; flexible spending \& health savings account; employee assistance program; and other company benefits.


To comply with pay transparency requirements and other statutes, you can notify us if you believe that a job posting is not compliant by completing this form.

jobs.title: Events Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
EvenUp is on a mission to close the justice gap using technology and AI. We empower personal injury lawyers and victims to get the justice they deserve. Our products enable law firms to secure faster settlements, higher payouts, and better outcomes for victims injured through no fault of their own in vehicle collisions, accidents, natural disasters, and more.


We are one of the fastest\-growing vertical SaaS companies in history, and we are just getting started. EvenUp is backed by top VCs, including Bessemer Venture Partners, Bain Capital Ventures, SignalFire, and Lightspeed. We are looking to expand our team with talented, driven, and collaborative individuals who seek to have a lasting impact. Learn more at www.evenuplaw.com.


As an Events Marketing Manager, you’ll be responsible for crafting, executing, and measuring in\-person hosted events to build and advance our sales pipeline across the United States. As the trusted advisor to marketing and sales leadership, you’ll have an in\-depth understanding of the nuances across regions and of the challenges our prospects and customers face. You’ll collaborate with cross\-functional marketing teams to ensure you actively share field feedback to influence upcoming campaigns while properly measuring success.

**What You'll Do**

* Plan, build, and execute event concepts and experiences targeting prospects and customers through our owned/hosted field marketing events program.
* Implement event programs while using your creativity and expertise to develop outstanding, scalable, and in\-person experiences to drive new business and upsells
* Forecast, measure, analyze, and report on the impact of demand creation activities on sales pipeline, revenue, and conversion against the agreed pipeline goals
* Own pipeline targets, marketing lead funnel after events, and conversion metrics for go\-to\-market, as well as the team’s efforts to improve and optimize our events programs for maximum ROI
* Communicate regularly with stakeholders on program results and findings for your events; aggregate results weekly, monthly, and quarterly for marketing and sales leadership
* Work on multiple, simultaneous events that require both strategic thinking and creativity, as well as high\-speed tactical execution
* Develop templated, scalable event programs that will grow with the company

**What You'll Bring**

* 3\+ years of proven experience in a B2B event marketing role
* Creative and innovative thinking with a willingness to take risks, iterate, and learn
* Owns event execution and leads others (direct and indirect) to meet deadlines while maintaining the quality of the work; proactively identifies and leads work streams to achieve goals
* Proven ability to drive cross\-functional collaboration across diverse sets of stakeholders
* Exceptional project and time management skills \- highly organized, detailed, focused, results\-driven, and accountable, with experience partnering with revenue and marketing operations teams
* Experience with planning and carrying out multiple, sophisticated marketing events and campaigns while working towards strategic goals
* Excellent interpersonal skills to deal professionally with senior executives, sales team members, vendors, and team members at all skill levels inside and outside the organization
* Self\-starter, highly motivated, and think through problems to come up with innovative solutions; excellent problem\-solving skills, strategic and analytical capabilities
* Proactively engage in forward\-thinking and innovative initiatives for the business and be proactive versus reactive
* Positive mindset and team player mentality
* Experience successfully managing third\-party vendors, contractors, and budgets (along with weekly reconciliation)
* Working knowledge of Salesforce CRM, Google Workspace, and other sales and marketing systems

**Notice to Candidates:**


EvenUp has been made aware of fraudulent job postings and unaffiliated third parties posing as our recruiting team – please know that we have no affiliation or connection to these situations. We only post open roles on our career page (evenuplaw.com/careers) or reputable job boards like our official LinkedIn or Indeed pages, and all official EvenUp recruitment emails will come from the domains @evenuplaw.com, @evenup.ai, @ext\-evenuplaw.com, no\-reply@ashbyhq.com or no‑reply@canditech.io email addresses.


To ensure fairness and proper consideration, we do not accept resumes or expressions of interest via email or social media messages. If you’re interested in a role, please submit your application directly through our careers page.

*If you receive communication from someone you believe is impersonating EvenUp, please report it to us at talent\-ops\-team@evenuplaw.com. Examples of fraudulent domains include “careers\-evenuplaw.com” and “careers\-evenuplaws.com”.*

**Benefits \& Perks:**


As part of our total rewards package, we offer attractive benefits and perks to our employees, including:

* Choice of medical, dental, and vision insurance plans for you and your family
* Additional insurance coverage options for life, accident, or critical illness
* Flexible paid time off, sick leave, short\-term and long\-term disability
* 10 US observed holidays, and Canadian statutory holidays by province
* A home office stipend
* 401(k) for US\-based employees and RRSP for Canada\-based employees
* Paid parental leave
* A local in\-person meet\-up program
* Hubs in San Francisco and Toronto

*Please note the above benefits \& perks are for full\-time employees*

*EvenUp is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to diversity and inclusion in our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.*


Compensation Range: $100K \- $115K

jobs.title: Events Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
**Join Modulate as we pioneer the future of conversational intelligence.**  

Modulate is a frontier AI company building advanced voice intelligence technology that understands nuance, intent, and emotion in real time.  

  

Our platform helps enterprises protect customers and employees from fraud, deepfake voice attacks, abuse, and other emerging voice\-based threats. As we expand beyond gaming into financial services, retail, contact centers, and other enterprise verticals, we are scaling our go\-to\-market efforts and investing in field marketing as a key growth channel.  

We’re looking for an Events Marketing Manager to plan, execute, and scale Modulate’s in\-person marketing programs. This role will own the full lifecycle of events, including industry conferences, tradeshows, executive dinners, partner events, and account\-based activations. The ideal candidate has deep, hands\-on experience managing complex event logistics and partnering closely with Sales, Channel, Customer Success, and executive teams to drive pipeline and revenue.  

This is an execution\-heavy role for someone who is comfortable owning programs end to end, managing multiple stakeholders and vendors, and operating in a fast\-paced, high\-growth environment. **Your Impact**
As our Events Marketing Manager, you will play a direct role in shaping how Modulate shows up in the market as we expand into new enterprise verticals. You will own the in\-person moments where prospects, customers, partners, and analysts experience our technology firsthand, and where critical pipeline is created, influenced, and accelerated.* Drive measurable pipeline creation and acceleration by designing field and digital programs with clear commercial intent.
* Elevate Modulate’s credibility within AI\-focused, technical, and enterprise buyer communities through high\-impact event presence.
* Transform events from one\-off brand moments into repeatable, revenue\-generating growth channels.
* Strengthen cross\-functional alignment across Marketing, Sales, Partnerships, Customer Success, and Executive leadership around field strategy and execution.
* Build scalable field marketing infrastructure that supports expansion into new verticals and enterprise markets.

 **What You Will Do*** Own and execute Modulate’s field and digital event programs end\-to\-end, including major industry conferences, tradeshows, executive dinners, hackathons, targeted account activations, and virtual programs such as webinars and roundtables.
* Design and scale Modulate’s long\-term field marketing strategy, identifying and prioritizing the conferences, community programs, and digital events that best support expansion into new enterprise verticals and drive pipeline creation and acceleration.
* Drive pipeline generation through events by partnering with Sales and Business Development on target account outreach, invitation strategy, and meeting\-setting programs before and after key events.
* Lead operational execution of events, managing budgets, timelines, vendors, logistics, and on\-site execution, including booth operations, demos, executive meetings, and issue resolution.
* Measure and scale program impact, building repeatable processes and playbooks while tracking event performance, pipeline influence, opportunity acceleration, and overall ROI.

 **What We Are Looking For**  

* 4–5\+ years of hands\-on field marketing experience, with 7\+ years of overall B2B marketing experience.
* Experience marketing technically complex products or familiarity with AI\-focused conferences, developer communities, or emerging AI ecosystems strongly preferred.
* Proven experience owning full\-cycle event execution including event logistics, pre\-, mid\-, and post\-event marketing support, and managing multi\-vendor relationships.
* Experience across a range of event types, from large industry tradeshows to smaller executive and ABM events.
* Strong track record of partnering cross\-functionally with the broader GTM team to drive pipeline and revenue impact.
* Highly organized, detail\-oriented, and able to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
* Strong communication skills and ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, ambiguous, high\-growth environment.

 **Benefits**
At Modulate, we prioritize people and sustainability—because doing great work requires feeling supported and safe. Some of our key benefits include:* Competitive salary \+ equity\- Full health, dental, and vision coverage
* Flexible PTO, with a strong culture of taking it
* Weekly team lunches with dietary accommodations
* Hybrid work: core in\-office days with flexible remote options
* Regular leadership and industry learning sessions
* Support for career development and continued learning
* Up to 8 weeks work\-from\-anywhere policy
* A deeply inclusive, human\-centered culture

 **Pay Transparency**
Modulate believes in transparency as a cornerstone of equity and trust. Compensation for this role is based on seniority, skills, and experience.  

Salary: $100K–$125K
Equity: Offered
Other perks: HSA, FSA, 15 holidays, professional growth resources
with significant overlap or duplication from other submissions will not be considered. **About Modulate**  

Modulate is on a mission to make voice a force for good online. Our tools help communities thrive by proactively detecting toxic behavior, protecting user identity, and empowering safety teams. We’re trusted by leaders in gaming and beyond—and we’re growing fast.  

We believe that great cultures don’t just happen. That’s why we’ve built a foundation of intentional systems: from bias\-reducing hiring practices to transparent pay to tools that help teams collaborate across communication styles. At Modulate, we treat people like people—and we’re building technology that does the same. **Ready to join us?** Apply here or reach out directly—we’re excited to meet you. **A quick note as you apply**
We kindly ask that you apply through the website rather than emailing careers@modulate.ai.  

For the questions “Your fit for the role,” “Your values/goals,” and “Why Modulate?” on our application form, we kindly ask that you focus your responses on experiences and motivations that directly relate to the role.  

  

Please avoid including details that would reveal protected demographic information (such as race, age, gender, ethnicity, or sexual orientation), as we strive to assess all candidates equitably and without bias.  

We also want to hear your authentic voice. While it can be helpful to use tools to support your thinking, we ask that your responses reflect your own perspective. Applications  

  

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: Senior Regional Marketing Specialist
jobs.description:
**Get to know Okta**  

  

Okta is The World’s Identity Company. We free everyone to safely use any technology, anywhere, on any device or app. Our flexible and neutral products, Okta Platform and Auth0 Platform, provide secure access, authentication, and automation, placing identity at the core of business security and growth.  

  

At Okta, we celebrate a variety of perspectives and experiences. We are not looking for someone who checks every single box \- we’re looking for lifelong learners and people who can make us better with their unique experiences.  

  

Join our team! We’re building a world where Identity belongs to you.

**Title: Senior Regional Marketing Specialist**



Okta is looking for a motivated and detail\-oriented **Senior R****egional Marketing Specialist** to support the development, execution, and optimization of demand generation programs across our AMER Strat and SLED Regional Marketing teams. In this role, you’ll collaborate with cross\-functional teams to plan and execute impactful marketing initiatives that drive engagement and pipeline growth. From event coordination to digital programs and performance reporting, you’ll play a critical role in ensuring operational excellence and marketing success.


**Key Responsibilities:**


**Program Planning and Execution:** Collaborate with AMER Strategic and Public Sector Marketing and Sales teams to plan and implement demand generation programs, including online and offline campaigns. Support activities such as regional events, inbound and outbound digital marketing, and more.


**Team Collaboration and Communication:** Act as a liaison between the AMER regional marketing team and other segment marketers (Commercial, Enterprise) as well as the xDR teams. Ensure seamless information sharing and alignment across teams for program success.


**Project Management:** Partner with internal teams and external resources to support the creation of high\-quality campaign assets, including email copy and sequences, targeted offers, and marketing materials.


**Performance Reporting:** Track and analyze channel performance, program ROI, and marketing funnel health. Provide actionable insights to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of marketing campaigns by region.


**Operational Support:** Assist with Salesforce campaign creation, audience targeting via 6sense, and development of campaign briefs. Create enablement tools and materials for sales teams, including SDRs and Account Executives, to ensure program alignment and readiness.


**Qualifications:**


* 1\-2 years of experience in B2B tech marketing; experience in B2G is a plus.
* Familiarity with marketing and sales tools such as Salesforce, Splash, 6sense, Marketo, and Asana.
* Analytical mindset with strong data analysis and reporting skills.
* Previous experience in a sales development or sales support role is a plus.
* Excellent written, verbal, and presentation communication skills.
* Strong attention to detail, particularly in writing and editing content.
* Exceptional prioritization, organizational, and project management abilities.
* Willingness to travel as needed.


\#LI\-Hybrid



P24096\_3318119

jobs.title: Field Marketing Specialist
jobs.description:
At Webflow, we're building the world's leading AI\-native Digital Experience Platform, and we're doing it as a remote\-first company built on trust, transparency, and a whole lot of creativity. This work takes grit, because we move fast, without ever sacrificing craft or quality. Our mission is to bring development superpowers to everyone. From entrepreneurs launching their first idea to global enterprises scaling their digital presence, we empower teams to design, launch, and optimize for the web without barriers. We believe the future of the web, and work, is more open, more creative, and more equitable. And we're here to build it together.

  

We're looking for a Field Marketing Specialist to join our growing Events and Brand Marketing team \- someone who's ready to roll up their sleeves, contribute to and learn from a team of seasoned event professionals, and help bring Webflow's field marketing programs to life globally. This is a new role joining an established and talented team.  

  

This is an early seat on a small, high\-performing team that moves quickly and takes its work seriously. You'll get real ownership, real mentorship, and real exposure to what it looks like to build field marketing programs that drive pipeline — supported by experienced event and field marketing professionals across North America and EMEA. We'll invest in your growth, and we'll expect you to bring your initiative, creativity, and follow\-through to everything you touch.


**About the role:**
===================


* Location: Remote\-first (United States; BC \& ON, Canada)
* Full\-time
* Permanent
* Exempt

The cash compensation for this role is tailored to align with the cost of labor in different geographic markets. We've structured the base pay ranges for this role into zones for our geographic markets, and the specific base pay within the range will be determined by the candidate's geographic location, job\-related experience, knowledge, qualifications, and skills.  
* 
* + United States (all figures cited below are in USD and pertain to workers in the United States)
	+ - Zone A: $95,000 \- $115,000
		- Zone B: $91,000 \- $109,000
		- Zone C: $86,000 \- $103,000
	+ Canada (figures cited below are in CAD and pertain to workers in ON \& BC, Canada)
	+ - 108,000 \- 130,000

This role is also eligible to participate in Webflow's company\-wide bonus program. Target amounts are a percentage of base salary and vary by career level. Payouts are based on company performance against established financial and operational goals. Please visit our Careers page for more information on which locations are included in each of our geographic pay zones. However, please confirm the zone for your specific location with your recruiter.


* Application Information:
* + Application deadline: applications accepted on an ongoing basis until position is closed and filled
	
	This posting is for a new position  
	+
* Reporting to the Manager, Field Marketing


As a Field Marketing Specialist, NAMER, you'll…


* Support the planning, coordination, and execution of Webflow's NAMER field marketing programs — including third\-party sponsorships, executive dinners, roadshows, and partner events — with increasing ownership as you grow in the role
* Help build and maintain program timelines, run\-of\-show documents, vendor communications, and event logistics from pre\-event planning through post\-event wrap\-up
* Work closely with the NAMER sales team to support field program execution, ensuring strong pre\-event outreach, on\-site sales presence, and post\-event follow\-through
* Support budget tracking across NAMER programs — monitoring spend, flagging variances, and helping the team consistently deliver programs on or under budget
* Coordinate with vendors, agencies, and contractors to support event production, swag, shipping, and on\-site logistics
* Track and report on event performance metrics — including attendance, pipeline contribution, and engagement data — and contribute to post\-event analysis and recommendations
* Collaborate closely with the broader Events and Brand Marketing team, including EMEA\-based colleagues, to share learnings, align on best practices, and contribute to a globally consistent Webflow field marketing experience
* Work on our largest event programs, including our annual user conference Webflow Conf

**About you:**
==============



Requirements:


* 2\-4 years of experience in field marketing, event marketing, or a closely related marketing role, with specific experience in a sales\-led growth organization
* Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or a related field — or equivalent professional experience
* Hands\-on experience supporting or executing field marketing programs — event sponsorships, hosted events, roadshows, executive programs, and account\-based marketing (ABM)
* Working proficiency and knowledge of Salesforce and Marketo; demonstrable experience and ability to pull reports, create and manage programs, track leads, and support campaign setup
* Genuine enthusiasm for travel, with availability to support 20\+ events per year across North America and occasionally internationally


You'll thrive as a Field Marketing Specialist, NAMER if you:


* Are energized by variety — no two weeks look the same in field marketing, and that's exactly how you like it
* Bring genuine attention to detail — you're the person who catches the typo on the event banner before it ships and double\-checks the run\-of\-show the morning of
* Work well across functions — you're comfortable building relationships with sales, communicating up to stakeholders, and collaborating across a distributed team
* Are a natural learn and coachable — you want to learn from experienced teammates, and you're not afraid to ask questions when you need help or more information
* Are a natural self\-starter — you don't wait to be told what needs doing, you see it and you do it
* Take ownership seriously — when something has your name on it, you see it through from first draft to final debrief
* Stay curious and open to growth — actively building fluency in emerging technologies like AI to unlock creativity, accelerate progress, and amplify impact.

### **Our Core Behaviors:**


* **Build lasting customer trust.** We build trust by taking action that puts customer trust first.
* **Win together.** We play to win, and we win as one team. Success at Webflow isn't a solo act.
* **Reinvent ourselves.** We don't just improve what exists, we imagine what's possible.
* **Deliver with speed, quality, and craft.** We move fast because the moment demands it, and we do so without lowering the bar.

### **Benefits**


* **Ownership in what you help build.** Every permanent Webflower receives equity (RSUs) in our growing, privately held company.
* **Health coverage that actually covers you.** Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans for full\-time employees and their dependents, with Webflow covering most premiums.
* **Support for every stage of family life**. 12 weeks of paid parental leave for all parents and 6\+ weeks of additional paid leave for birthing parents. Plus inclusive care for family planning, menopause, and midlife transitions.
* **Time off that's actually off.** Flexible vacation, paid holidays, and a sabbatical program to help you recharge and come back inspired.
* **Wellness for the whole you.** Access to mental health resources, therapy and coaching.
* **Invest in your future.** A 401(k) with 100% employer match (up to $6,000/year) in the U.S., and support for retirement savings globally.
* **Monthly stipends that flex with your life.** Localized support for work and wellness expenses — from Wi\-Fi to workouts.
* **Bonus for building together.** All full\-time, permanent, non\-commission employees are eligible for our annual WIN bonus program.


*Temporary employees may be eligible for paid holiday and time off, statutory leaves of absence, and company\-sponsored medical benefits depending on their Fixed Term Contract and their country/state of employment.*


### **Remote, together**



At Webflow, equality is a core tenet of our culture. We are an Equal Opportunity (EEO)/Veterans/Disabled Employer and are committed to building an inclusive global team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, beliefs, and experiences. Employment decisions are made on the basis of job\-related criteria without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other classification protected by applicable law. Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Webflow will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.


### **Stay connected**



Not ready to apply, but want to be part of the Webflow community? Consider following our story on our Webflow Blog, LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and/or Glassdoor.


### **Please note:**


*We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Upon interview scheduling, instructions for confidential accommodation requests will be administered.*


*To join Webflow, you'll need a valid right to work authorization depending on the country of employment.*


*If you are extended an offer, that offer may be contingent upon your successful completion of a background check, which will be conducted in accordance with applicable laws. We may obtain one or more background screening reports about you, solely for employment purposes.*


*For information about how Webflow processes your personal information, please review* *Webflow's Applicant Privacy Notice**.*

jobs.title: Senior Campaign Manager
jobs.description:
**About Kyra**
--------------


We run influencer marketing for large brands. Not one\-off posts. Structured programs designed to drive real business outcomes.


We use data and AI to improve creator selection and performance forecasting, but this role isn’t about buzzwords. It’s about running strong accounts, delivering consistently, and growing revenue year over year. We’ve scaled quickly. Now we need someone who knows how to manage enterprise clients properly.


As **Senior Campaign Manager**, you’ll be the engine that powers Kyra’s creator campaigns from start to finish. You’ll manage day\-to\-day campaign delivery, ensuring that everything runs on time, on budget, and to the highest creative standard. From briefing creators to reporting back to clients, you’ll bring structure, energy, and problem\-solving to every campaign you touch.

**What You’ll Be Doing**
------------------------


**Campaign Management**

* Own day\-to\-day management of influencer marketing campaigns across multiple brands
* Oversee campaign timelines, budgets, deliverables, and approvals
* Be the main point of contact for creators, clients, and internal teams


**Client Service**

* Build trusted relationships with clients by delivering clear, proactive communication
* Anticipate client needs and ensure campaigns exceed expectations


**Creator Collaboration**

* Source, brief, and manage creators throughout campaign lifecycles
* Ensure content meets brand guidelines, cultural nuance, and campaign objectives


**Reporting \& Optimisation**

* Track campaign performance using Kyra’s proprietary platform and analytics tools
* Provide actionable insights and recommendations for future campaigns

**What You Bring**
------------------


**Experience \& Skills**

* 4\-6 yrs experience in influencer marketing, talent management, or campaign/project management
* Negotiation experience w/ creators
* Creator contracting experience
* Strong written\-communication skills
* Must be able to work US hours (EST/CT/PST)
* Strong organisational prioritizations skills with the ability to juggle multiple projects at speed
* Confident communicator who can manage stakeholders with ease


**Industry Knowledge**

* Strong cultural competency and knowledge of trends for US market
* Understanding of social media platforms, creator culture, and digital marketing
* Passion for the creator economy and emerging cultural trends


**Mindset \& Drive**

* Proactive problem\-solver who thrives in a fast\-paced environment
* Collaborative, adaptable, and committed to high standards

**Sound like you?**


We’d love to hear from campaign managers who are ready to step up and deliver impactful creator marketing at scale.

**Benefits**
------------

**We’re a remote\-first team**, giving you the flexibility to work from wherever you do your best work  

**Annual Time Off:** 25 days PTO \+ bank holidays, 4 company\-wide days off (Kyra Days), 3 wellness days, and paid sick leave each year  

**Growth:** Annual Learning \& Development budget to invest in your skills.  

**Rewards:** Annual bonus scheme to recognise great work  

**Healthcare coverage** for US\-based team members


AI is core to how we operate. Use it to elevate your work. But everything in your application should reflect your own judgement and experience.

**Support**


If you have a condition or require an adjustment to our interview process \- please let us know so we can make adjustments to support you. Please note that when you submit an application or resume, your data will be processed in line with our **privacy policy**.


Compensation Range: $75K \- $85K

**Position: Field Marketing Specialist**


Be part of a fast\-paced team where hard work is recognized and success is celebrated. This is a **field\-based role** where you will connect with homeowners, introduce our services, and schedule appointments for our Sales Representatives. This is an active, outdoor position — not an office, call center, or remote scheduling job.


We are looking for positive, motivated individuals who enjoy being active, communicating with people, and working toward goals in an outdoor environment.

**Responsibilities:**

* Knock doors in assigned neighborhoods
* Talk with homeowners and set appointments for sales team
* Follow a simple scripts and closer process
* Contribute to a strong team environment

**What You Get:**

* Fast Weekly Pay
* Weekly Bonuses
* Training and Mentorship
* Fast\-Track advancement into full Sales Representative (based on performance)


If you’re motivated, outgoing, and ready to grow, we’d love to hear from you. Apply today!
