jobs.title: DIrector, Field Marketing
jobs.description:
**About IntelePeer.ai:**


IntelePeer delivers rapidly deployable communications solutions for an always connected world. Our Conversational AI Platform instantly improves your customers’ communications experience. IntelePeer provides industry leading time\-to\-value with Agentic AI solutions that work seamlessly with your infrastructure. Our no\-code templates, low\-code, co\-creation, and developer API options provide you with simple, easy\-to\-use tools that can be utilized by anyone.

**Job Summary:**


The Director of Field Marketing is a strategic, results\-oriented leader responsible for driving qualified pipeline, accelerating deal cycles, and supporting regional sales across IntelePeer’s core industries—with a strong emphasis on healthcare. This role partners closely with Sales to build targeted field initiatives, ABM programs, and customer engagement motions designed specifically for healthcare providers, payers, MSOs, DSOs, and complex care networks.


This leader will also manage and mentor one direct remote team member, ensuring high performance, tight alignment with Sales, and consistent execution across regions.

 **Responsibilities:**

* Build and execute field programs that drive qualified pipeline in priority regions, with a dedicated focus on healthcare verticals.
* Develop integrated, vertical\-specific campaigns aligned with healthcare needs—patient experience, care coordination, contact center automation, and RCM workflows.
* Own field execution for ABM, events, partner activations, roadshows, workshops, and executive\-level engagements.

**Sales Partnership \& Enablement**

* Serve as the primary marketing partner for Healthcare Sales leaders.
* Translate sales insights into actionable marketing plans that support both new logo growth and expansion.
* Deliver follow\-up frameworks, playbooks, and tailored content to enable AE engagement and accelerate late\-stage pipeline.

**Remote Team Leadership**

* Lead, coach, and develop a remote field marketing team with clear goals, aligned workflows, and measurable performance expectations.
* Manage prioritization, resource allocation, and quality standards across distributed team members.
* Establish strong communication rhythms and accountability structures for remote execution.

**Event Leadership \& Customer Engagement**

* Own strategy, budget and execution for all field events, with emphasis on high\-value healthcare conferences, partner events, and executive roundtables.
* Oversee event logistics, onsite presence, and post\-event pipeline processes.
* Ensure exceptional experience delivery for healthcare decision\-makers.

**Measurement \& Performance**

* Track and report program performance including pipeline creation, influence, velocity, and ROI.
* Partner with Marketing Ops for attribution accuracy, insights, and continuous improvement.
* Optimize field plans based on data, revenue goals, and sales feedback.

**Required Qualifications**

* 8\+ years of B2B marketing experience, including 5\+years in field marketing or ABM supporting enterprise or healthcare sales.
* Proven success generating pipeline and influencing revenue in healthcare technology or enterprise SaaS.
* Experience in managing and developing remote teams.
* Strong partnership skills with Sales at all levels.
* Demonstrated excellence in event strategy, healthcare\-focused programs and ABM execution.
* Data driven operator with reporting and optimization skills.

**Education \& Experience:**

* Minimum Education: Bachelor’s degree (BA/BS) in Marketing, Business, or a related field.
* Preferred Education: MBA or master’s degree in a relevant field, reflecting strategic, high\-level business acumen.
* Industry Certifications: While not always required, certifications in Account\-Based Marketing (ABM) (e.g., from Demand base) or specialized healthcare marketing credentials are highly advantageous.
* Functional Knowledge: Strong, demonstrated understanding of healthcare industry regulations, such as HIPAA, and familiarity with healthcare technology workflows (e.g., EMR, RCM, or telehealth).

**Competencies:**·


. Strategic Thinking

* Sales Partnership Excellence
* Remote Team Leadership
* Operational Rigor
* Data Fluency
* Event \& Experience Leadership
* Communication \& Influence

**Physical Requirements:**

* Sedentary work lifting no more than 10 pounds.
* Occasional lifting, carrying, and standing.
* Frequent hand/eye coordination to operate office equipment.
* Vision sufficient to read computer screens, reports, and related department documents.
* Dexterity to operate computer keyboards and other related office equipment.
* Endurance sufficient to sit and work at a computer for extended periods of time.
* Frequent speech communication and hearing.

**Why you'll love it here:**

* Unlimited Vacation for exempt employees
* Paid Holidays
* Competitive medical, dental \& vision insurance for employees and their dependents
* 401K Retirement Plan
* Stock Options
* Company\-paid life insurance
* Health \& Flexible Savings Accounts
* Cell phone, gym, and internet reimbursement
* Paid Parental Leave
* Tuition Reimbursement
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
* Free snacks (Denver, and or Fort Lauderdale)
* Fun events (virtual and in\-person)

 ***Applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S.***  

***We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment visa at this time.***

*Any requests to exercise your rights as a data subject under GDPR should be submitted to infosec@intelepeer.com for prompt processing. Please refer to our Privacy Policy (at* *www.intelepeer.com/privacy/intelepeer\-privacy\-policy**) for any questions on how IntelePeer complies with GDPR.*

*For California residents only: Please refer to the link below for IntelePeer’s Applicant CCPA Privacy Notice.* *https://intelepeer.com/privacy/intelepeer\-california\-applicant\-privacy\-notice/*

*IntelePeer participates in E\-Verify.*

  

At IntelePeer, we value diversity and are proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or any other protected status.

  

We strive to provide reasonable accommodations to applicants and employees with disabilities to support them in performing the essential functions of their roles.


If you have any questions or need assistance, please contact our Director of Recruiting.

jobs.title: Integrated Marketing Segment Lead
jobs.description:
The Opportunity
  
Are you ready to lead a dynamic segment at one of the world's most innovative software companies? Adobe is seeking an exceptionally talented Integrated Marketing Segment Lead to design and drive a multi\-channel marketing strategy for a specific business segment. Ideallly based in San Jose, CA, this role is perfect for a strategic problem solver with a startup attitude who thrives in a large\-scale matrix corporate environment.
  
What you'll Do
  
Own the strategic marketing roadmap for the segment, defining the Route\-to\-Market marketing strategy based on GTM priorities and identifying growth opportunities.
  
Serve as the primary Point of Contact for Sales, GTM, and Product Marketing, ensuring that coordinated marketing plans align with B2B Adobe goals
  
Partner with cross\-functional teams to develop persona\-based journeys and buying committee strategies that cater to the segment's outstanding lifecycle needs.
  
Translate segment needs into actionable briefs for various Centers of Excellence, including Content, Paid Media, Email, and Events, ensuring flawless execution.
  
Drive the feedback loop between Marketing and Sales, collaborating with Sales Operations and GTM to enable regional initiatives and pipeline acceleration.
  
Monitor segment\-specific performance metrics, including MQA, Pipe Contribution, and Conversion Rates, using deep analytical insights to optimize the funnel.
  
What you need to succeed
  
10\+ years of experience in Coordinated Marketing, Campaign Strategy, or Segment Management within a B2B technology environment.
  
Proven ability to navigate and influence within highly matrixed, global organizations.
  
Experience building programs from the ground up, with a focus on diagnosing operational gaps and implementing scalable strategies.
  
Strong data approach, comfortable with CRM and analytics platforms for data\-backed decision\-making.
  
Ability to thrive in an agile, startup\-style environment, demonstrating high adaptability and a bias for action.
  
Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field, with equivalent experience accepted; MBA or equivalent experience preferred.
  
About Adobe
  
Adobe empowers everyone to create through innovative platforms and tools that unleash creativity, productivity and personalized customer experiences. Adobe’s industry\-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity.
  
  

Our 30,000\+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours.
  
  

Let’s Adobe together
  
At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture , focus on people, purpose and community , Adobe for All , comprehensive benefits programs , the stories we tell , the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create.
  
  

Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Learn more.
  
  

Adobe aims to make our Careers website and recruiting process accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call \+1 408\-536\-3015\.
  
  

**AI Use Guidelines for Interviews:**  

Our interviews are designed to reflect your own skills and thinking. The use of AI or recording tools during live interviews is not permitted unless explicitly invited by the interviewer or approved in advance as part of a reasonable accommodation. If these tools are used inappropriately or in a way that misrepresents your work, your application may not move forward in the process.
  
  

At Adobe, we empower employees to innovate with AI — and we look for candidates eager to do the same. As part of the hiring experience, we provide clear guidance on where AI is encouraged during the process and where it’s restricted during live interviews. See how we think about AI in the hiring experience .
  
  

**Expected Pay Range:** Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $112,000 \-\- $215,100 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job\-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process.
  
  

In California, the pay range for this position is $148,500 \- $215,100
  
  

At Adobe, for sales roles starting salaries are expressed as total target compensation (TTC \= base \+ commission), and short\-term incentives are in the form of sales commission plans. Non\-sales roles starting salaries are expressed as base salary and short\-term incentives are in the form of the Annual Incentive Plan (AIP).
  
  

In addition, certain roles may be eligible for long\-term incentives in the form of a new hire equity award.
  
  

**State\-Specific Notices:**  

**California :**  

Fair Chance Ordinances
  
Adobe will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment in accordance with state and local laws and “fair chance” ordinances.
  
  

**Colorado:**  

Application Window Notice
  
  

If this role is open to hiring in Colorado (as listed on the job posting), the application window will remain open until at least the date and time stated above in Pacific Time, in compliance with Colorado pay transparency regulations. If this role does not have Colorado listed as a hiring location, no specific application window applies, and the posting may close at any time based on hiring needs.
  
  

**Massachusetts:**  

Massachusetts Legal Notice
  
It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

jobs.title: Field Marketing Coordinator
jobs.description:
**Lifetime Home Remodeling** is seeking a highly organized **Field Marketing Coordinator** to join our growing California operations based out of our **Escondido office**. This vital position supports our lead generation programs across retail, canvassing, and event channels, ensuring our Brand Ambassadors are properly staffed, equipped, and supported. This is an administrative\-focused role with occasional in\-field presence, ideal for someone who thrives in a fast\-paced, multi\-tasking environment and is passionate about operational excellence.


**What’s in it for You:**


**Competitive Pay:** Hourly wage of $22–$25 per hour based on experience, with opportunities for career advancement as Lifetime expands across California.  

**Supportive Culture:** Join a collaborative and fast\-growing team that values transparency, accountability, and team success.  

**Benefits Package:** Full health, dental, and vision coverage, wellness benefits such as ClassPass, a competitive 401(k) with company match, and generous PTO policy.  

**Career Growth:** Be a key contributor to a nationally ranked home remodeling company with internal growth opportunities across departments and markets.


**Schedule:**


**Full\-Time Hours:** Monday through Friday, with flexibility to occasionally visit weekend events or retail activations as needed.


**Key Responsibilities:**


**Staffing \& Scheduling:** Maintain and update schedules for California Brand Ambassadors across all activation types (Retail, Events, Canvassing, Home Shows). Coordinate shift coverage, track hours, and support payroll accuracy.  

**Operations \& Logistics:** Track uniform inventory, mileage reports, marketing supplies, and ensure all activation teams are prepared and equipped.  

**Retail \& Partner Coordination:** Maintain communication with store partners like Costco, ensuring professionalism and performance. Help support potential expansion into Lowe’s and Home Depot.  

**Compliance:** Reinforce code of conduct and field expectations through reminders, training coordination, and partner feedback loops.  

**Materials Management:** Organize, distribute, and track branded marketing materials, ensuring kits and displays are activation\-ready and replenished.  

**CRM \& Reporting:** Use CRM systems to support lead attribution accuracy and performance reporting. Help ensure correct commission data entry.  

**Administrative Support:** Assist with onboarding, training material organization, uniform distribution, and general field team support in collaboration with leadership.


**Qualifications:**


**Education:** High school diploma or equivalent required.


**Administrative Skills:** Strong attention to detail, organizational abilities, and comfort with managing multiple workflows at once.  

**Communication:** Clear and professional written and verbal communication, capable of interfacing with store managers, retail partners, and internal staff.  

**Experience:** Familiarity with retail or event marketing environments, lead generation processes, or CRM platforms preferred.  

**Tech\-Savvy:** Comfortable using Microsoft Office Suite and CRM platforms to support operations and reporting.  

**Compliance:** Ability to pass a background check and uphold high standards of professionalism and integrity.


**Physical Requirements:**


**Mobility:** Ability to sit for extended periods and occasionally lift up to 75 lbs.  

**Travel:** Willingness to occasionally travel to local retail or event sites as needed for support and logistics.


**Join Us:**



As a **Local Field Marketing Coordinator**, you will play a critical behind\-the\-scenes role in helping Lifetime’s California lead generation teams succeed. If you're proactive, detail\-oriented, and ready to grow with a high\-performing team, we encourage you to apply today and be part of the Lifetime difference.

**Vianney Vocations**
---------------------


**Tallahassee, FL (HYBRID POSITION)**
-------------------------------------

3491173990

$70k to $80k Salary
Send applications or inquiries to: **sam@vianneyvocations.com**


  
### **Job Description**


Help inspire the next generation of Catholic priests! Vianney Vocations is seeking a talented project manager to spearhead various campaigns in dioceses across the country. This includes financial appeals and vocation\-related campaigns.  

  

If you are energized by meaningful work, thrive on bringing projects to completion, and want your skills to directly impact the future of the Church, we would love to hear from you.  

  

\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+  

  

Mission Alignment:  

Candidates must have a sincere desire to serve the Church by promoting priestly and religious vocations. This is not simply a management role; it is ministry through professional excellence.  

  

\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+  

  

What You Will Do:  

You will be helping Vocation Offices in their mission to increase their number of seminarians. Working within our team, you will take ownership of Seminarian Funding Appeals and Called by Name campaigns, ensuring smooth execution and wide participation.  

  

Core Responsibilities:  

* Project Management for 15\+ campaigns annually
* Collaborate with clients (Vocation Directors and diocesan teams) to plan and run campaigns from conception to completion
* Write compelling copy for letters, emails, and other communication pieces
* Coordinate communications with a team of graphic designers and web experts.
* Manage campaign web pages
* Design and deploy email and SMS campaigns
* Coordinate timelines and deliverables across multiple projects
* Track performance and suggest improvements for future campaigns
* Manage data for tracking and reporting
* Contribute creative ideas for increasing participation and engagement

  

\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+  

  

We Are Looking For Someone Who:  

* Is mission\-driven and excited to serve the Church
* Brings a positive, professional presence to client relationships
* Has experience working on high\-level projects within the Church
* Is highly organized and adept at handling multiple projects at once
* Communicates clearly and persuasively in writing
* Enjoys collaborating with clients and guiding projects to completion

  

\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+  

  

Preferred Qualifications:  

Top candidates will demonstrate many of the following:  

* Proven experience promoting events or campaigns
* Strong writing and editing skills
* Digital marketing experience (email, web, social, SMS)
* Project management experience across multiple simultaneous initiatives
* Understanding of the Catholic Church and vocation ministry
* Strong professional references

  

\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+  

  

Location \& Work Arrangement:  

Preferred: On\-site at our home office in beautiful Tallahassee, Florida  

Remote work considered for exceptional candidates  

  

\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+\+  

  

Compensation \& Start Date:  

Competitive salary based on experience, with opportunities for rapid advancement and increased compensation based on performance.  

Start date is flexible: April\-May 2026
Send applications or inquiries to: **sam@vianneyvocations.com**


### **About the Employer**


Vianney Vocations helps dioceses inspire men to consider priesthood and increase their number of seminarians.  

  

As we expand our mission, especially through Vocation Pathway, we are looking for talented and committed Catholic professionals.  

  

We are a small but highly effective team who work with dioceses, parishes, and schools across the country. Ideal candidates will be proactive, reliable, and passionate about working in a mission\-driven environment.  

  

We begin each day with prayer, and are committed to a positive, uplifting work experience, great relationships with colleagues, and most all, meaningful work that makes a real impact within the Church.

jobs.title: Integrated Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
Midi Health is looking for an experienced Integrated Marketing Lead to support the planning and execution of high\-impact, cross\-channel campaigns across brand, product, RX, and supplements.



Integrated Marketing at Midi serves as the connective tissue across marketing and cross\-functional teams — ensuring that campaigns are strategically grounded, clearly messaged, and executed in a coordinated way across channels.



This role is ideal for someone who thrives in complexity, sees campaigns as systems rather than isolated channel efforts, and can bring structure, clarity, and momentum to fast\-moving launches.


**What You'll Do**



Campaign Strategy \& Architecture


* Translate brand and product strategy into integrated campaign plans
* Define campaign narrative, messaging hierarchy, and channel roles
* Develop integrated briefs and GTM frameworks for major launches (brand moments, RX initiatives, supplement launches, etc.)
* Ensure campaigns ladder to the Midi brand platform and defined business objectives


Cross\-Channel Coordination


* Partner closely with Brand, Growth, Lifecycle, Organic Social, Influencer, PR, Partnerships, Product Marketing, and Clinical stakeholders
* Align teams around campaign objectives, timelines, messaging, and sequencing
* Develop workbacks and ensure asset readiness across teams
* Drive clear communication and alignment throughout the campaign lifecycle


GTM Planning \& Launch Excellence


* Support integrated launch planning across major company initiatives
* Identify dependencies, risks, and gaps early and proactively
* Ensure campaigns roll out cohesively across owned, paid, and earned channels
* Contribute to leadership\-ready summaries and campaign updates


Campaign Performance \& Learning


* Partner with channel owners to synthesize campaign\-level performance
* Evaluate campaigns against defined objectives
* Identify learnings and translate them into actionable recommendations for future initiatives


**Who You Are**


* 6–10\+ years of experience in integrated marketing, campaign strategy, product marketing, or cross\-channel marketing roles
* Experience leading or coordinating multi\-channel campaigns in high\-growth environments
* Strong understanding of GTM planning and messaging systems
* Highly organized with the ability to manage complex timelines and multiple stakeholders
* Strong cross\-functional communicator who can influence without direct authority
* Analytical and business\-minded, with the ability to connect campaign strategy to business outcomes
* Comfortable operating in ambiguity and building process where needed


Midi Health offers a competitive base salary of $130\-140K (dependent on experience, location and other factors), equity grants, benefits (medical, dental, vision), work\-from\-home stipends and flexible time off.



\#LI\-MB1


At this time, Midi is unable to provide visa sponsorship. **All Candidates must be authorized to work in the United States without current or future sponsorship needs.**



Please note that all official communication from Midi Health will come from an **@joinmidi.com** email address. We will **never** ask for payment of any kind during the application or hiring process. If you receive any suspicious communication claiming to be from Midi Health, please report it immediately by emailing us at **careers@joinmidi.com**.



Midi Health is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to pay equity and ensure that all qualified applicants receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or protected veteran status. Our compensation philosophy is based on fair, objective criteria and the impact of the role, regardless of an applicant's salary history.



Please find our CCPA Privacy Notice for California Candidates here.

jobs.title: Manager, Field Marketing
jobs.description:
**Company Description**  

Amer Sports is a sporting goods company with internationally recognized brands including Salomon, Arc’teryx, Peak Performance, Atomic and Wilson.


“The Mountain Sport Company”, Salomon was born in 1947 in the heart of the French Alps and the birthplace of modern alpinism. Salomon’s commitment to innovative design and passion for mountain sports created a vast range of revolutionary new concepts in bindings, boots, skis and apparel for both Alpine and Nordic skiing and brought innovative solutions to footwear, apparel and equipment for snowboarding, adventure racing, mountaineering, hiking, trail running, and many other sports.


Through performance driven design, Salomon delivers innovation and progression to mountain sports; converting new ideas into action and expanding the limits of possibility. Salomon’s heritage, culture, and commitment are tied together by one simple concept: the world’s leading mountain people creating the world’s leading mountain products. Salomon is responsibly committed towards the outdoor through its sustainability program. Diversity is one of Salomon’s five values, therefore we are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all.


Salomon is headquartered in Annecy, France.

 **Job Description** **POSITION TITLE**: Field Marketing Manager

**REPORTS TO**: VP Marketing, Salomon NAM

**BUSINESS AREA**: Marketing

**LOCATION:** Ogden, USA

**TRAVEL:** 50%

**OVERVIEW**


The Field Marketing Manager is responsible for delivering a consistent, and elevated consumer experience in epicenters. They are directly responsible for managing and developing the Field Marketing program and ensuring omnichannel execution across all categories in scope. This role works directly with sales directors, agencies, Salomon retail teams, trade teams and other partners to drive demand, aligned with campaigns, in epicenters. The Field Marketing Manager is responsible for the return on investment of our field marketing program. To do this successfully, this role will be constantly auditing, evolving and instilling best practices in the field. Going hand in hand, this position is responsible for gathering consumer insights, industry trends, competitor benchmarking and product feedback through their team.


This role is responsible for supporting sell\-through and driving consumer demand across the US, with a focus on epicenters. This position will own the overall playbook and toolkit for field marketing and ensure masterplan and calendar coordination with all activity.

**SCOPE OF WORK:** Salomon categories: Running, Outdoor, Sportstyle

**ESSENTIAL DUTIES \& RESPONSIBILITIES**


To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty and responsibility satisfactorily:


Team Leadership and People Management

* Manage the US field marketing team
* Create the field marketing program scorecard by implementing KPIs directly related to the Salomon strategy
* Responsible for the field marketing team’s calendar and schedule
* Provide guidelines and tracking tools for budget management
* Directly responsible for coaching, feedback, reviews and career planning support
* Ensure excellent collaboration and communication with sales and retail teams
* Recommend and implement tools and platforms to track KPIs and relevant activity
* Vehicle management


In\-Store Experience/ Trade Marketing

* This role manages the Associate Marketing Manager who is responsible for training. The Field Marketing Manager will ensure the handoff to the trade marketing team, retail and sales teams and consistently assess the effectiveness of the training. While the Associate Marketing Manager is responsible for training, this role has direct accountability for this function.
* Work closely with the trade marketing team. This includes the hand over of the seasonal in\-store marketing playbook. The Field Marketing Manager is responsible for working with the trade team to ensure training, demos, seeding and VM needs align with our masterplan calendar.
* Work with the trade team to make sure we are defining and supporting the right accounts and doors aligned with our wholesale strategy.
* Co\-Create sell\-through contests
* Ensure point\-of\-purchase displays and merchandising guidelines reflect our “shopper experience framework” and work to equipe teams with relevant POP materials
* Recommend swag and other giveaways that would add value to the field marketing team


Community Marketing

* Work closely with the Sport and Community Managers to design the community playbook for the field marketing team
* Recommend new partnerships with clubs and other groups that align with the strategy and the brand to build the core
* Manage the epicenter ambassador and product seeding program. This includes working with the merchandising team to make sure key products are seasonally aligned on. This role with recommend the seeding product assortment
* Manage and budget for demo fleets, event materials and any other tool kit materials for field activation
* Collaborate on event design, organization and execution including sponsored and owned event activation
* Support focus groups needed


 Tracking and Reporting

* Manage and ensure adherence to KPI scorecard. Put platforms and tools in place that support clear reporting on field marketing activity
* Responsible for field marketing input on quarterly business review presentations and other deliverables as requested
* Actively assess and pull reporting from our epicenter dashboard. Share information across channels in a monthly format
* Work with sales and category to understand seasonal business plans by epicenter and be up to date on channel reporting and new distribution points as applicable
* Manage travel expenses and local marketing budget as assigned


Additional Collaboration and Support

* Deliver epicenter lead season playbooks and toolkits for the US and Canada aligned with brand marketing seasonal objectives
* Collaborative event planning required. This includes field marketing training events, sales meetings, summits and other regaion camps
* This role is responsible for creating clarity on roles and responsibilities as teams come together
* Ensure that the Associate Marketing Manager has what they need to deliver the training tool kits on time and in full
* Connect on a biweekly cadence with retail marketing team to ensure field marketing support at Salomon retail. This includes event support, loyalty program activations and the coordination (through Associate Marketing Manager) of training.

 **Qualifications**  

8\-12 years practical experience in the running, outdoor, fashion and/or winter sports industry is preferred. Retail experience a plus.


Must have experience managing large teams

 **Additional Information** **PHYSICAL DEMANDS \& WORKING ENVIRONMENT**


The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to talk and hear. The employee is frequently required to sit, stand and walk. The employee must occasionally kneel, stoop, crouch, twist and lift to 30 pounds, with or without accommodation.


Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision, and ability to adjust focus.


The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.


The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate.

jobs.description:

**Job Summary**  

The Senior Manager, Regional Marketing is responsible for providing leadership, driving top\-line performance, and brand consideration at the local level by leveraging Papa Johns national campaigns, promotions, and marketing strategies. The role is responsible for measuring the market impact for local campaigns and the impact on key business objectives. The role helps to execute the broader marketing strategy at the local level, working with agencies and operators to drive brand engagement. This role is responsible for driving marketing strategies at the local level in alignment with the broader business strategy and performance targets.

* Serve as the subject matter expert for local marketing, consultant for franchisees and as strategic guidance for agency partners.
* Lead local agencies in quarterly planning for assigned markets; providing calendar, budget, and national media and promotional updates to develop localized recommendations.
* Analyze campaign performance, sales trends, and key market dynamics to build recommendations based in data and insight.
* Monitor and share competitive activity in assigned markets to identify opportunities and strategic advantages for the market, franchisees and local agency.
* Provide oversight and guidance on new store opening plans and in market activations.
* Collaborate cross\-functionally with Operations, Accounting/Finance, and all Marketing functions
* Ability to manage effectively and oversee a support manager role within your region providing oversight, guidance, and measurable objectives.

jobs.title: Growth Marketing Manager, ABM & Field
jobs.description:
Overview:

Our Culture and Impact


Cvent is a leading meetings, events, and hospitality technology provider with more than 5,000\+ employees and 24,000\+ customers worldwide, including 60% of the Fortune 500\. Founded in 1999, Cvent delivers a comprehensive event marketing and management platform for marketers and event professionals and offers software solutions to hotels, special event venues and destinations to help them grow their group/MICE and corporate travel business. Our technology brings millions of people together at events around the world. In short, we’re transforming the meetings and events industry through innovative technology that powers the human connection.


Cvent's strength lies in its people, fostering a culture where everyone is encouraged to think like entrepreneurs, taking risks and making decisions confidently. We value diverse perspectives and celebrate differences, working together with colleagues and clients to build strong connections.


AI at Cvent: Leading the Future


Are you ready to shape the future of work at the intersection of human expertise and AI innovation? At Cvent, we’re committed to continuous learning and adaptation—AI isn’t just a tool for us, it’s part of our DNA. We’re looking for candidates who are eager to evolve alongside technology. If you love to experiment boldly, share your discoveries, and help define best practices for AI\-augmented work, you’ll thrive here. Our team values professionals who thoughtfully integrate AI into their daily work, delivering exceptional results while relying on the human judgment and creativity that drive real innovation.


Goldcast, which is now a part of Cvent, is seeking a strategic, hands\-on Growth Marketing Manager, ABM \& Field to own the strategy, execution, and pipeline impact of our enterprise account\-based marketing programs. This role focuses on designing and running multi\-touch ABM programs that engage high\-value accounts across digital, sales, and experience\-led channels.


This is not an event logistics role. Field events and executive experiences are one component of a broader ABM strategy, used selectively to deepen relationships and accelerate deals. You will partner closely with Sales to identify and prioritize target accounts, develop account\-specific engagement strategies, and drive measurable pipeline progression by translating intent, engagement, and pipeline signals into coordinated marketing and sales plays.


In This Role, You Will::
* Own and execute Goldcast’s ABM strategy in partnership with Sales, aligning programs to ICP and revenue goals.
* Build, manage, and continuously refine target account lists, segmenting and tiering accounts based on signals, pipeline stage, and revenue potential.
* Translate account insights and intent signals (e.g., 6sense, Clay, Common Room) into coordinated account plans and conversion plays.
* Lead multi\-channel ABM initiatives across digital ads (1:1 and 1:few), sales activation, gifting, content, and curated experiences, including targeting, messaging, and follow\-up.
* Develop scalable ABM playbooks that Sales can operationalize to drive consistent engagement with target accounts.
* Build and activate account\-specific journeys that nurture stakeholders from awareness through opportunity creation and pipeline acceleration.
* Partner with Sales on account plans, outreach sequencing, and personalized engagement strategies.
* Identify gaps in account engagement and deploy targeted campaigns to re\-engage stalled or dormant accounts.
* Incorporate field events and executive experiences as strategic ABM touchpoints for priority target accounts.
* Define target account and attendee strategies for select high\-impact events and experiences.
* Partner with Sales to turn event participation into structured pre\-event outreach, post\-event follow\-up, and pipeline movement.
* Ensure field programs are integrated into broader account journeys rather than operated as standalone campaigns.
* Attend select events, support Sales engagement, and facilitate introductions with target accounts.
* Develop and optimize conversion plays that turn event engagement into qualified opportunities.
* Own reporting on account engagement, SALs, discovery meetings, pipeline creation, and efficiency across target segments.
* Maintain accurate reports and dashboards that track pipeline impact and performance of ABM and field programs.
* Regularly analyze performance data, share insights with marketing and sales leadership, and recommend optimizations.
* Contribute to quarterly planning, experimentation, and continuous improvement of ABM and field strategies.


Here's What You Need::
* Bachelor’s degree.
* 7\+ years of B2B demand generation, ABM, or field marketing experience.
* SaaS experience strongly preferred.
* Experience supporting enterprise or mid\-market sales motions.
* Proven ability to manage named account lists and account\-based programs.
* Demonstrated track record of partnering with Sales to drive pipeline.
* Strong analytical mindset with experience maintaining dashboards, reports, and performance reviews.
* Comfort being directly accountable to pipeline and revenue\-related outcomes.
* Strong understanding of experience\-led marketing and executive engagement.
* Ability to analyze data, identify trends, and translate insights into clear actions.
* Comfort working cross\-functionally with Sales, Marketing Operations, and Product Marketing.
* Highly organized and detail\-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple initiatives in parallel.

jobs.title: Senior Manager Field Marketing Activation
jobs.description:
**Job Summary**  

The Senior Manager of Field Marketing Activation leads the strategy, development, and execution of multi\-channel marketing programs that drive brand awareness, guest engagement, and revenue growth at the local level. This role bridges brand strategy, creative, media, digital, and field marketing to ensure seamless, insight\-driven campaigns that deliver measurable business outcomes.


This position is responsible for overseeing all local agency partners to ensure seamless collaboration, excellence, and high\-quality output across creative, media, digital, PR, free\-lancers, and internal partner teams. The role ensures agencies are aligned with brand objectives, delivering on KPI’s, and operating efficiently within scope, budget, and timeline. The position serves as the strategic bridge between internal stakeholders and external partners to drive brand consistency, innovation, and business growth.  

  

 **Duties and Responsibilities (other duties as assigned)**

* Build and oversee the integrated field marketing strategy across commercial, digital, social, media, CRM, and promotional channels.
* Align field activation plans with business KPI’s, guest insights, and revenue targets.
* Serve as the primary point for all local agencies, managing day\-to\-day relationships and long\-term strategic partnerships.
* Evaluate agency performance regularly, ensuring alignment on objectives, budgets, and deliverables. Set up each to report quarterly post buy insights.
* Translate business objectives into agency briefs and ensure clarity, accuracy and strategic cohesion between external agencies and internal partners.
* Partner with internal teams\- Commercial brand team, National Media \& eCRM team, Operations, Finance, Product, Insights, etc. on strategies to cascade the national promotions down to the local level.
* Oversee scope of work, negotiate contracts, manage budgets, and invoices for accuracy.
* Effectively drives brand consideration at a local level through the adoption and implementation of national marketing strategies
* Provide strategic partnership to both internal leaders and external partners.
* Implement tools or systems for tracking performance, reporting, and deliverables.
* Builds effective working relationships with advertising agencies and key operators to drive engagement and support of brand strategies
* Hold agencies and franchisees accountable for compliance and delivery of the brand’s strategic plan at the local level.
* Execute local programs that deliver against business goals and support the broader national plan in partnership with the Operations Excellence team.
* Serve as subject matter expert (SME) on regularly scheduled regional calls, providing relevant marketing updates and key field initiatives.
* Facilitate development of commercial pipeline through test market recruitment, engagement, and support.
* Stay informed of market trends and customer preferences to identify innovation opportunities at the regional/local level.

jobs.title: Manager, Regional Marketing
jobs.description:
**Job Summary**  

The Manager, Regional Marketing is responsible for providing leadership and top\-line performance by implementing local marketing initiatives across markets of responsibility. The role is responsible for using marketing analysis and insights to understand business performance and develop local market plans to grow the business.

**Duties and Responsibilities (other duties as assigned)**

* Manage local agencies in the development and implementation of coop wide and store\-level marketing initiatives including media, customer relationship management, offer management, creative, and local store marketing. Ensure brand standards are maintained
* Serve as marketing partner and resource for franchisees and other key stakeholders
* Assist local agencies in annual and quarterly planning for assigned markets; provide calendar, budget, and national media updates to adequately develop recommendations
* Analyze campaign performance, sales trends, and market insights to present to co\-ops and franchisees and assist in providing solutions to optimize results
* Monitor competitive activity in assigned markets and identify opportunities to share with regional leadership and local agencies
* Support new store openings and on\-site market activations
* Collaborate cross\-functionally with Operations, Accounting/Finance, and all Marketing functions
* Serve as subject matter expert in markets of responsibility by socializing market initiatives, performance, and best practices to senior marketing leaders

jobs.title: Event Marketing Manager
jobs.description:
### **Description**

  

At Hallmark Health Care Solutions, what we do and how we do it go hand in hand. We bring a relentless focus to both our products and our people—grounded in **curiosity, a bias for action, accountability and ownership, strong judgment, and a willingness to challenge the status quo** in pursuit of better outcomes.
  

Hallmark Health Care Solutions is led by passionate experts with an average of 20\+ years’ experience in IT, nursing, process engineering, finance, and healthcare. We are committed to strengthening communities by producing technologies, support services, and thought leadership that tangibly improve patient care and community health outcomes.  

Hallmark Health Care Solutions is seeking an Event Marketing Manager who thrives at the intersection of strategy, execution, and creativity. This role is ideal for a hands‑on event marketer who knows how to turn live and virtual experiences into powerful drivers of brand awareness, demand generation, and revenue growth.  

  

You’ll play a critical role in shaping how Hallmark shows up in the market — designing memorable event experiences that help us stand out, connect meaningfully with prospects and customers, and “punch above our weight” at industry events.
  

This position requires a strong blend of strategic thinking, operational excellence, and cross‑functional collaboration, working closely with marketing, sales, leadership, vendors, and external partners.  

### **What You’ll Do**

  

Event Strategy \& Planning* Develop and own Hallmark’s event marketing strategy, including a recommended portfolio of live, virtual, and hybrid events aligned to target audiences and business objectives.
* Evaluate and select events based on audience relevance, visibility opportunities, and ROI potential.
* Define how Hallmark shows up at each event — from booth presence and sponsorships to speaking engagements and experiential moments.

Event Execution \& Management* Lead end‑to‑end planning and execution for all tradeshows, sponsored events, webinars, and client events.
* Serve as the primary liaison with event organizers, vendors, and internal stakeholders to ensure seamless execution.
* Manage logistics across all event phases, including:
	+ Registration, shipping, booth setup, and teardown
	+ Inventory management for booth assets and materials
	+ On‑site coordination and troubleshooting
	+ Post‑event follow‑up and evaluation
* Utilize project management tools to track timelines, deliverables, and dependencies across teams.
* Partner with internal creative resources or external agencies to develop all event collateral and branded materials.
* Attend events as needed to support execution, lead capture, and on‑site coordination.

Demand Generation \& Lead Management* Own accountability for marketing‑qualified leads generated through events.
* Partner with marketing and sales teams to ensure leads are properly scored, routed, nurtured, and tracked.
* Ensure tight alignment between pre‑event promotion, on‑site engagement, and post‑event follow‑up.

Webinars \& Virtual Events* Manage all aspects of webinar marketing, including speaker coordination, registrations, hosting logistics, and follow‑up.
* Collaborate with subject matter experts and marketing partners to deliver engaging, high‑quality virtual experiences.

Cross‑Functional Collaboration* Coordinate closely with Sales, Marketing, and other internal teams for event preparation, messaging alignment, and feedback loops.
* Participate in planning and debrief sessions to continuously improve event effectiveness.

Budget \& Advisory Programs* Manage the event marketing budget, including sponsorships, travel, materials, and vendor costs.
* Create, curate, and maintain a Customer Advisory Board, partnering with Sales to select participants and coordinating multiple advisory sessions annually (virtual and/or in‑person).

  

### **What We’re Looking For**

  

* 5\+ years of experience in event marketing, tradeshow coordination, or event management
* Strong understanding of event‑driven demand generation and ROI measurement
* Experience with marketing automation and CRM systems (Salesforce preferred)
* Confident, polished communicator with strong interpersonal skills
* Comfortable engaging with stakeholders at all levels, including C‑suite executives
* Highly organized, detail‑oriented, and able to manage multiple projects simultaneously
* Strong financial management and vendor negotiation skills
* Ability to perform well under pressure and adapt quickly in live event environments
* Willingness to travel as needed
* Ability to lift up to 25 lbs. with or without accommodation

Our preference is someone who can work a hybrid role in our Dallas office, 3 days a week.### **The Perks**

  

* Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance with Employee Premiums Covered by HHCS at 100% and Company Cost Share for any Dependents Enrolled
* $3000 Annual Company Contributions to HSAs for all Employees Enrolled in the HSA Eligible Health Plan
* Unlimited Paid Time Off
* Pre\-Tax and Roth 401(K) Retirement Options
* On\-Site Gym, Free Parking, and Provided Lunches 3 Times per Week in the Dallas Office!

Hallmark Health Care Solutions, Inc is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All employment decisions are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to race, color, religion or belief, national, social or ethnic origin, sex, age, physical, mental or sensory disability, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, marital, civil union or domestic partnership status, past or present military service, family medical history or genetic information, family or parental status, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate. HHCS reserves the right to amend the job description, duties or qualifications based on company needs.
  

By applying to Hallmark Health Care Solutions, your application and all supplemental information is subject to our Privacy Policy. When clicking “Apply”, you will be redirected to our Applicant Tracking System, Pinpoint.  

### **About Hallmark Healthcare Workforce Technology**


We specialize in delivering innovative solutions and exceptional services to meet the diverse needs of our clients. With a strong commitment to quality and customer satisfaction, we strive to exceed expectations and drive success in every project we undertake.

jobs.title: Event Marketing Demand Generation Senior Manager
jobs.description:
*To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.*

Job Category


Marketing \& Communications
Job Details


**About Salesforce**

Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.


Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.


Salesforce is seeking a strategic, audience\-centric Senior Manager to serve as a demand generation lead for our events portfolio. This role is responsible for shaping and orchestrating the end\-to\-end campaign strategy that connects priority audiences to Salesforce’s most strategic event experiences, driving measurable impact across registrations, attendance, engagement, and pipeline.


The Events Demand Generation team produces industry\-defining campaigns that represent the ultimate expression of the Salesforce brand, innovation, and customer success. As a Senior Manager, you will operate as the demand generation leader—translating event objectives into integrated audience, segmentation, and channel strategies, and ensuring coordinated execution across paid, owned, earned, and partner channels.


This role sits at the center of a highly cross\-functional ecosystem, partnering closely with Experience Strategy, Product Marketing, Creative, Messaging, Media Lab, Lifecycle Marketing, Regional Marketing, Analytics, and executive stakeholders. You will ensure every campaign component works in concert to reach the right audiences, deliver meaningful engagement, and achieve pipeline and business impact goals.


This is a strategic role requiring strong campaign ownership, audience and channel expertise, and the ability to align diverse teams around a shared demand generation strategy.


**Responsibilities:**
=====================

**Campaign Leadership \& Strategic Ownership**
----------------------------------------------

* Serve as the demand generation lead for priority Salesforce events, owning campaign strategy from audience definition through execution and performance.
* Translate event objectives into integrated, audience\-first campaign strategies that drive registrations, attendance, engagement, and pipeline.
* Develop comprehensive campaign frameworks outlining audience segments, messaging alignment, channel roles, sequencing, and activation strategy.
* Ensure campaigns are executed cohesively across paid, owned, organic, and partner channels.

**Audience Strategy, Segmentation \& Targeting**
------------------------------------------------

* Define and evolve audience segmentation strategies using behavioral, firmographic, technographic, and engagement data.
* Identify priority audiences, whitespace opportunities, and high\-value segments aligned to event and business objectives.
* Partner with analytics and data teams to continuously refine targeting, improve conversion rates, and optimize audience reach and efficiency.
* Ensure consistent audience strategy alignment across all campaign channels and touchpoints.

**Cross\-Functional Campaign Orchestration**
--------------------------------------------

* Act as the central liaison across Experience Strategy, Creative, Messaging, Product Marketing, Media Lab, Lifecycle Marketing, and regional teams.
* Ensure campaign strategy is clearly translated into channel activation plans and creative execution.
* Partner with Experience Strategy to align audience strategy with event positioning, content, and attendee experience design.
* Collaborate with creative and messaging teams to ensure campaign assets resonate with priority audiences and align to campaign goals.
Align channel owners across paid media, email, web, organic social, and partner channels to ensure coordinated campaign execution.  
* 

**Performance Ownership \& Optimization**

* Own campaign performance across key metrics including registrations, attendance, engagement, and pipeline generation.
* Monitor campaign performance, identify trends and gaps, and proactively drive optimizations.
* Deliver performance reporting, insights, and strategic recommendations to stakeholders and leadership.
* Apply data\-driven insights to continuously refine segmentation, targeting, channel mix, and campaign strategy.

**Stakeholder Leadership \& Executive Alignment**
-------------------------------------------------

* Serve as the strategic point of contact for demand generation campaign strategy across internal stakeholders.
* Align senior stakeholders and cross\-functional partners around campaign priorities, timelines, and goals.
* Present campaign strategy, performance, and recommendations to senior marketing leadership.
* Drive clarity, alignment, and accountability across complex, multi\-channel campaign efforts.

**Campaign Innovation \& Best Practice Development**
----------------------------------------------------

* Identify opportunities to improve campaign effectiveness through new approaches to targeting, segmentation, and channel orchestration.
* Contribute to the evolution of Salesforce’s event demand generation frameworks, playbooks, and best practices.
* Stay current on evolving demand generation strategies, audience targeting capabilities, and marketing technologies.

**Minimum Qualifications**
==========================

* 8\+ years of experience in demand generation, integrated campaign strategy, or event marketing, preferably in B2B technology
* Proven experience owning and leading integrated marketing campaigns across multiple channels
* Strong expertise in audience segmentation, targeting strategy, and campaign orchestration
* Experience working cross\-functionally with creative, messaging, channel owners, and senior stakeholders
* Strong analytical skills and experience using data to drive campaign optimization and strategy
* Excellent communication, leadership, and stakeholder management skills
* Strong project management and organizational capabilities
* Ability to operate effectively in fast\-paced, highly cross\-functional environments

**Preferred Qualifications**
============================

* Experience leading demand generation strategy for large companies and campaigns. Plus if for large\-scale events, conferences, or tentpole campaigns
* Experience working in enterprise B2B technology marketing
* Familiarity with Salesforce audiences, products, and ecosystem
* Experience aligning campaign strategy to pipeline generation and business impact metrics
* Strong executive presence and experience presenting to senior stakeholders
* Experience working with marketing analytics, audience platforms, and marketing automation tools

**About Salesforce:** Who we are, what we do, how we do it?


Founded in 1999, Salesforce is the global leader in Customer Relationship Management (CRM). Companies of every size and industry are using Salesforce to transform their businesses, across sales, service, marketing, commerce, and more by connecting with customers in a whole new way. We harness technologies that can revolutionize companies, careers, and, hopefully, our world.


Salesforce is built on a set of four core values: Trust, Customer Success, Innovation, and Equality. By making technology more accessible, we're helping create a future with greater opportunity and equality for all. We strive to build a workplace that reflects society and where everyone feels seen, valued, heard, and empowered to create the best work of their careers. Learn more about our commitment to Equality at https://www.salesforce.com/company/equality/ . This has taken our company to great heights, including being named one of the FORTUNE "100 Best Companies to Work For®" 2020 — 12 years in a row.


There are those who choose to work with the best and brightest. And then, there are those who want to do more than just a job. They are the ones improving lives, not only their careers. Having an impact now instead of later. Doing something that’s so much bigger than themselves, an industry, and their company.


We believe everyone can be a Trailblazer. Join Salesforce and discover a future of new opportunities.


Unleash Your Potential


When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.


Accommodations


If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form .


Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.


Posting Statement


Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.


In the United States, compensation offered will be determined by factors such as location, job level, job\-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Certain roles may be eligible for incentive compensation, equity, and benefits. Salesforce offers a variety of benefits to help you live well including: time off programs, medical, dental, vision, mental health support, paid parental leave, life and disability insurance, 401(k), and an employee stock purchasing program. More details about company benefits can be found at the following link: https://www.salesforcebenefits.com.
At Salesforce, we believe in equitable compensation practices that reflect the dynamic nature of labor markets across various regions.\&\#xa;\&\#xa;The typical base salary range for this position is $143,400 \- $216,900 annually. \&\#xa;\&\#xa;The range represents base salary only, and does not include company bonus, incentive for sales roles, equity or benefits, as applicable.

* Working knowledge of integrated marketing channels and funnel strategies across paid, owned, and organic channels.
* Data\-driven and results\-oriented mindset, with experience analyzing campaign performance and identifying optimization opportunities.
* Familiarity working with Salesforce Sales Cloud, Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Tableau, Google Analytics, and Excel.
* Strong understanding of marketing and sales funnel dynamics, including audience acquisition and pipeline contribution.
* Cross\-functional team player with the ability to collaborate effectively across multiple stakeholders and teams.
* Ability to quickly grasp company messaging, brand positioning, and audience value propositions.
* Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills.
* Ability to work independently while balancing multiple priorities and stakeholder needs.
* Curious, proactive, and growth\-oriented mindset.

jobs.description:
United States
**About Prove**
===============



As the world moves to a mobile\-first economy, businesses need to modernize how they acquire, engage with and enable consumers. Prove’s phone\-centric identity tokenization and passive cryptographic authentication solutions reduce friction, enhance security and privacy across all digital channels, and accelerate revenues while reducing operating expenses and fraud losses. Over 1,000 enterprise customers use Prove’s platform to process 20 billion customer requests annually across industries, including banking, lending, healthcare, gaming, crypto, e\-commerce, marketplaces, and payments. For the latest updates from Prove, follow us on LinkedIn.



Prove is driving the future of digital identity. We are looking for Provers who know how to make an impact. We’re talking self\-starting professionals who thrive in a fast\-paced environment, process information quickly, and make intelligent decisions. The work is challenging and requires not only smart but natural curiosity and tenacity. Teamwork is also important to us – we work together and play together.



Prove has big plans, and we’re excited about the future. If this sounds like the place for you – come join our team!

  

**Department: Growth Marketing**


**Reports To:** **Senior Director, Global Marketing**


**FLSA Status: Exempt**


**Location: US Remote**

### 


### **Role Overview**



We’re seeking a strategic and commercially minded Director, Field Marketing manager to own and evolve our field marketing strategy across priority regions. This role is accountable for driving pipeline, accelerating revenue, and scaling high\-impact field programs, while directly managing and developing our Field Marketing Managers.



This is a senior, hands\-on role for someone who can move seamlessly between strategy and execution, act as a trusted partner to Sales and Marketing leadership, and bring rigor to how we measure and optimize field ROI.


### **Key Responsibilities**


#### **Regional Strategy \& Revenue Impact**


Own the regional field marketing strategy, aligned to pipeline, revenue, and account\-based growth objectives  

* 

Act as the primary marketing stakeholder for Sales leadership, with clear accountability for field\-sourced and field\-influenced pipeline  
* 

Translate GTM priorities into scalable, high\-impact field programs that support demand creation, deal acceleration, and customer expansion  
* 

#### **Field \& Event Leadership**


Oversee the end\-to\-end strategy and execution of flagship events, tradeshows, executive roundtables, customer events, and virtual field programs  

* 

Balance large\-scale programs with high\-touch ABM motions (1:1, 1:few) focused on priority accounts  
* 

Continuously test and evolve event formats, channels, and technologies to improve engagement and ROI  
* 

#### **People Management \& Team Development**


Directly manage and mentor Field Marketing Managers, providing clear direction, coaching, and development support  

* 

Set priorities, operating rhythms, and performance expectations for the field function  
* 

Build repeatable processes, templates, and best practices to support future team growth  
* 

#### **Cross\-Functional Partnership**


Partner closely with Sales, Product Marketing, Partner Marketing, and Demand Gen to ensure consistent messaging and integrated execution  

* 

Collaborate with SDR and Sales teams to enable effective pre\- and post\-event follow\-up  
* 

Influence senior stakeholders with clear insights, recommendations, and business cases  
* 

#### **Budget, Measurement \& Optimization**


Own the regional field marketing budget, ensuring disciplined investment in high\-ROI initiatives  

* 

Define success metrics and analyze pipeline, revenue impact, and conversion performance across the funnel  
* 

Use data and insights to continuously optimize programs and inform future strategy  
* 

### **Experience \& Skills**


7–10\+ years of B2B field marketing or demand generation experience within a high\-growth SaaS or technology environment  

* 

Proven success owning regional field marketing strategy and driving measurable pipeline and revenue impact  
* 

Experience leading complex event portfolios including tradeshows, executive events, and ABM\-driven field programs  
* 

Prior people\-management experience, with a track record of coaching and developing high\-performing marketers  
* 

Strong understanding of B2B sales cycles and close alignment with Sales and SDR teams  
* 

Data\-driven mindset with experience using CRM and marketing automation platforms (e.g. Salesforce, HubSpot/Marketo)  
* 

Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and executive\-level presentation skills  
* 

Highly organized, proactive, and comfortable operating in a fast\-paced, evolving environment  
* 
* Willingness to travel for key field events as required

  



This position description should not be considered the final description of the position. The position description is not intended to be an all\-inclusive list of duties and standards of the positions. It should be assumed that we would, to some extent, structure responsibilities in accordance with the successful candidate’s capabilities and changing business conditions. Incumbents will follow any other instructions, and perform any other related duties, as assigned by their supervisor.



The anticipated salary range for this role is $180,000 \- $200,000 plus variable commission / company bonus. Offered salary will be determined by the applicant’s education, experience, knowledge, skills, geo\-location and abilities, as well as internal equity and alignment with market data.


**Benefits \& Perks for FTE Provers:**

  

* Competitive salaries \& Bonus Plan (for eligible roles) and Equity Plan
* Modern Health for financial, mental, and physical wellness
* 401(k) Retirement Plan \& Match (US Offices) and Local Country Pension (International Offices)
* Unlimited Vacation and Flexible hours
* Comprehensive medical benefits for you and your family ❤️
* Emotional \& Physical Wellness – Access to wellness services (EAP \& Prove Well\-Being Reimbursement)
* Bottomless snacks \& beverages for certain office locations
* Daily GrubHub stipend for lunch if coming into the office (US Offices)
* A great place to work and connect with other talented Provers like yourself!


Don’t meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that women and people of color are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Prove we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.


**Equal Opportunity Employment:**  

  

Prove is an equal opportunity employer committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all people regardless of race, color, religion, gender or sexual orientation, age, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, veteran status or other personal characteristics


**Privacy \& Data Protection:**  

  

When you are applying for a job at Prove, we collect and use your personal information in the job application process. To understand more about how Prove uses your personal information, please see our Recruitment Privacy Policy on our website.

  

**Create a Job Alert**
Interested in building your career at Prove? Get future opportunities sent straight to your email.
